The District Supply Officer,
[District]

Subject: Complaint regarding denial of PDS ration entitlement

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], am a ration card holder (Card No. [Card No.], Category [APL/BPL/AAY]) residing at [Address]. The Fair Price Shop at [Location] has refused to provide my monthly ration entitlement for the months of [Months] without valid reason.

I request your immediate intervention to ensure my food entitlement is provided and appropriate action is taken against the Fair Price Shop dealer.

Enclosures: Ration card copy, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]
[Mobile Number]

category: सरकारी योजना और सार्वजनिक सेवा समस्याएं
subcategory: Pension Delay or Non-Payment (Old Age / Widow / Disability Pension)
problem_description: An eligible beneficiary is not receiving their government pension (old age, widow, disability) despite being registered, or the pension has stopped without reason.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Verify your registration and status on the National Social Assistance Programme (NSAP) portal.
Step 2 – Check if your bank account and Aadhaar are linked correctly for Direct Benefit Transfer.
Step 3 – File a written complaint with the Gram Panchayat or Municipality.
Step 4 – Visit the Block Development Office or District Social Welfare Office.
Step 5 – File a complaint on the state social welfare portal or CPGRAMS.
Step 6 – Contact the Collector Office if the BDO is unresponsive.
Step 7 – File an RTI to know the status of your pension application.
Step 8 – Approach DLSA for legal aid if systemic denial is occurring.
required_documents: Age proof (for old age pension) or death certificate of husband (widow pension)
escalation_path: Gram Panchayat → Block Development Office → District Social Welfare → District Collector → State Department → CPGRAMS
complaint_template:
To,
The District Social Welfare Officer,
[District]

Subject: Complaint regarding non-payment of [Old Age / Widow / Disability] Pension

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], am a registered beneficiary of [Pension Scheme Name] (Registration No. [Number]) residing at [Address]. Despite my eligibility and registration, I have not received my pension since [Month/Year].

I request your office to investigate the delay and ensure regular payment of my pension immediately.

Enclosures: Aadhaar card, age/disability/widow proof, bank passbook

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]
[Mobile Number]

category: सरकारी योजना और सार्वजनिक सेवा समस्याएं
subcategory: PM Awas Yojana / Housing Scheme Complaint
problem_description: A beneficiary approved under PM Awas Yojana (rural or urban) has not received their housing grant, or a housing official is demanding a bribe for processing the application.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Verify your beneficiary status on the PM Awas Yojana portal (pmayg.nic.in or pmaymis.gov.in).
Step 2 – Check if your Aadhaar and bank account are correctly linked.
Step 3 – File a complaint with the Gram Panchayat or Municipal Office.
Step 4 – Escalate to the Block Development Officer or Urban Local Body.
Step 5 – File a complaint on the PMAY portal's grievance section.
Step 6 – File on CPGRAMS if the state-level response is inadequate.
Step 7 – Report bribery demands to the Anti-Corruption Bureau and Vigilance Department.
Step 8 – File an RTI to know the status of your application and fund release.

authorities.name: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs

escalation_path: Gram Panchayat/ULB → BDO → District Collector → State Government → CPGRAMS → Ministry
complaint_template:

[Mobile Number]

category: सरकारी योजना और सार्वजनिक सेवा समस्याएं
problem_description: A student belonging to SC/ST/OBC/Minority community or economically weaker section has not received their government scholarship despite eligibility, or their scholarship application was rejected unfairly.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Check your scholarship application status on the National Scholarship Portal (scholarships.gov.in).
Step 2 – Contact your institution's scholarship coordinator for status.
Step 3 – File a written complaint with the District Social Welfare Officer.
Step 4 – Escalate to the State Scholarship Authority or Social Welfare Department.
Step 5 – File a complaint on CPGRAMS.
Step 6 – Contact the National Commission for SC/ST if discrimination is involved.
Step 7 – File an RTI application to obtain information about your application status.

authorities.name: State Social Welfare / Minority Affairs Department

escalation_path: Institution → District Social Welfare → State Department → CPGRAMS → Ministry → National Commission
complaint_template:

The District Social Welfare Officer,

I request your office to investigate and ensure the scholarship amount is credited to my account at the earliest.

Enclosures: Application acknowledgment, caste certificate, income certificate, Aadhaar

Yours faithfully,

[Date]
[Mobile Number]

category: सरकारी योजना और सार्वजनिक सेवा समस्याएं
problem_description: A citizen belonging to SC/ST/OBC is unable to obtain their caste certificate from government authorities despite being eligible, or the certificate is being unreasonably delayed.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Submit a formal application for a caste certificate at the Tehsildar or Revenue Office with all supporting documents.
Step 2 – Obtain the acknowledgment receipt of your application.
Step 3 – If no response within the stipulated time (usually 15-30 days), file a complaint with the Tehsildar.
Step 4 – Escalate to the Sub-Divisional Magistrate if Tehsildar is unresponsive.
Step 5 – File a complaint on CPGRAMS or the state's public grievance portal.
Step 6 – File an RTI application asking about the status of your certificate.
Step 7 – Approach the National Commission for SC or National Commission for Backward Classes.
required_documents: Father/mother's caste certificate or school records showing caste

authorities.name: National Commission for Scheduled Castes / Tribes

escalation_path: Tehsildar → SDM → District Collector → CPGRAMS → National Commission for SC/ST
complaint_template:

[Mobile Number]

problem_description: A landlord is forcibly evicting a tenant without following legal eviction procedures, cutting off electricity/water, or threatening the tenant to vacate the premises.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Collect your rent agreement and all rent receipts.
Step 2 – Document the landlord's actions (threats, utility disconnection) with evidence.
Step 3 – Send a legal notice to the landlord through a lawyer stating your right to remain.
Step 4 – File a police complaint against the landlord for forcible eviction and property trespass.
Step 5 – File a complaint with the Rent Controller (in states with Rent Control Acts).
Step 6 – Approach the Civil Court for an injunction against the illegal eviction.
Step 7 – Contact DLSA for free legal aid.
escalation_path: Police → Rent Controller → Civil Court → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Station House Officer,
[Police Station Name]

Subject: Complaint regarding illegal forced eviction by landlord

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], am a tenant at [Address] under a valid rental agreement dated [Date] with my landlord [Landlord Name]. My landlord is illegally trying to evict me by [describe: threatening / disconnecting utilities / changing locks] without following the legal eviction process.

I request police intervention to stop this illegal eviction and protect my legal right to continue occupancy.

Enclosures: Rent agreement, rent receipts, evidence of landlord's actions

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]
[Mobile Number]

subcategory: Landlord Refusing to Return Security Deposit
problem_description: A landlord is refusing to return the security deposit paid by the tenant at the start of the tenancy, even after the tenant has vacated the property and fulfilled all obligations.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Collect your rent agreement, receipts for security deposit payment, and vacating confirmation.
Step 2 – Send a written legal notice to the landlord demanding return of the deposit within 30 days.
Step 3 – If the landlord does not respond, file a consumer complaint at the Consumer Commission.
Step 4 – Alternatively, file a civil suit for recovery of money in Small Causes Court or Civil Court.
Step 5 – In states with Rent Control Acts, file a complaint with the Rent Controller.
Step 6 – Approach DLSA for free legal representation.
required_documents: Vacating proof (keys returned, final rent receipts)
escalation_path: Legal Notice → Rent Controller → Civil Court → Consumer Commission
complaint_template:
To,
The Civil Judge / Small Causes Court,
[District]

Subject: Recovery suit for security deposit

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], was a tenant at [Address] under a rental agreement dated [Date] with landlord [Landlord Name]. I vacated the premises on [Date] and fulfilled all obligations. I paid a security deposit of Rs. [Amount] which the landlord has refused to return despite a legal notice dated [Date].

I request the court to direct the landlord to return the security deposit of Rs. [Amount] with interest.

Enclosures: Rent agreement, deposit receipt, legal notice, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

problem_description: A landlord is facing the problem of a tenant who refuses to pay rent for several months despite being provided with accommodation.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Send a written demand notice to the tenant for all overdue rent.
Step 2 – Issue a legal eviction notice under the applicable Rent Control Act or Transfer of Property Act.
Step 3 – File an eviction suit in the Rent Controller's Court or Civil Court for non-payment of rent.
Step 4 – Also file a suit for recovery of arrears of rent.
Step 5 – Obtain an eviction order from the court.
Step 6 – Execute the eviction order through the court with police assistance if tenant refuses to vacate.
Step 7 – Contact DLSA for free legal representation.

authorities.name: Local Police (for execution of court order)

escalation_path: Legal Notice → Rent Controller → Civil Court → High Court
complaint_template:

The Rent Controller / Civil Court,

Subject: Eviction petition for non-payment of rent

I request the court to pass an eviction order and direct recovery of arrears of rent.

Enclosures: Rent agreement, demand notice, property documents

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

problem_description: A landlord is repeatedly entering the tenant's rented premises without prior notice or consent, violating the tenant's right to peaceful enjoyment and privacy.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Document each incident of unauthorized entry with date, time, and witnesses.
Step 2 – Send a written notice to the landlord asserting your right to peaceful enjoyment.
Step 3 – File a police complaint for trespass if the landlord enters by force.
Step 4 – File a complaint with the Rent Controller citing breach of lease terms.
Step 5 – Approach the Civil Court for an injunction restraining the landlord.
Step 6 – Consult DLSA for free legal advice.
required_documents: Incident log with dates and witnesses
escalation_path: Police Complaint → Rent Controller → Civil Court
complaint_template:

The Station House Officer,
[Police Station Name]

Subject: Complaint regarding unauthorized entry by landlord

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], am a tenant at [Address] under a valid rent agreement. My landlord [Landlord Name] has been entering my premises without prior notice or permission on [dates], violating my right to privacy and peaceful enjoyment.

I request police intervention and appropriate action to stop this harassment.

Enclosures: Rent agreement, incident log, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]
[Mobile Number]

problem_description: A citizen notices illegal or unauthorized construction in their area that violates building bye-laws, encroaches on public land, or poses a safety risk.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Note the details of the illegal construction including address, type of construction, and approximate start date.
Step 2 – Photograph the construction and any visible violations.
Step 3 – File a complaint with the local Municipal Corporation / Gram Panchayat.
Step 4 – If no action is taken within 30 days, escalate to the District Urban Development Department.
Step 5 – File a complaint with the Town Planning Authority.
Step 6 – File a complaint on CPGRAMS or the state grievance portal.
Step 7 – File a Public Interest Litigation (PIL) in High Court if the construction is widespread and authorities are non-responsive.
required_documents: Your own identity proof and address proof
escalation_path: Municipal Corporation → Town Planning Authority → District Urban Development → State Government → High Court (PIL)
complaint_template:
To,
The Building and Construction Department,
[Municipal Corporation / Gram Panchayat Name]

Subject: Complaint regarding illegal construction

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], residing at [Address], wish to report an illegal construction being carried out at [Location / Address of Construction]. The construction appears to be [unauthorized / in violation of setback rules / on public land / structurally unsafe].

I request your authority to inspect the construction and take necessary action including stopping the illegal construction and demolishing unauthorized portions.

Enclosures: Photographs, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]
[Mobile Number]

problem_description: A citizen is experiencing excessive noise from a factory, loudspeakers, construction site, vehicle horns, or public events, violating Noise Pollution (Regulation and Control) Rules, 2000.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Document the noise problem: note source, approximate decibel level, time of day, and frequency.
Step 2 – Approach the source of noise and request them to stop or reduce noise.
Step 3 – File a complaint with the local police station if noise is from events or loudspeakers.
Step 4 – File a complaint with the Pollution Control Board for industrial or commercial noise.
Step 5 – File a complaint with the Municipal Corporation for construction noise.
Step 6 – Approach the District Magistrate who has powers to regulate loudspeakers and noise.
Step 7 – File a complaint on the State Pollution Control Board's online portal.
required_documents: Written complaint with description of noise source
escalation_path: Police / Municipal → State Pollution Control Board → District Magistrate → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Sub-Inspector / Station House Officer,
[Police Station Name]

Subject: Complaint regarding noise pollution

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], residing at [Address], wish to report that [Source of Noise: factory/event/vehicle/construction] at [Location] has been creating excessive noise [continuously / during late nights / during prohibited hours] since [Date].

This noise is causing health problems and disturbing our peaceful living in violation of the Noise Pollution Rules, 2000.

I request immediate action to stop or control the noise.

Enclosures: Identity proof, written complaint, recordings (if any)

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]
[Mobile Number]

problem_description: Industrial effluents, sewage, or chemical waste is being discharged into rivers, ponds, or groundwater, affecting drinking water quality and public health.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Document the pollution: photograph effluent discharge, note the source, and record visible effects.
Step 2 – Collect water samples if possible and have them tested at a government lab.
Step 3 – File a complaint with the State Pollution Control Board.
Step 4 – File a complaint with the local Municipal Corporation or Gram Panchayat.
Step 5 – Report to the District Collector if it is causing a public health crisis.
Step 6 – File a complaint with the Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) for serious violations.
Step 7 – File a PIL in the High Court for remedial action and compensation.
required_documents: Map or description of pollution source
escalation_path: Gram Panchayat → State Pollution Control Board → CPCB → District Collector → High Court PIL
complaint_template:

The Regional Officer,
State Pollution Control Board,
[Region]

Subject: Complaint regarding water pollution by industrial discharge

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], residing near [Location], wish to report that [Industry / Factory Name] at [Address] is discharging untreated effluents into [River / Pond / Groundwater] since [Date]. This is causing serious water contamination and health problems for our community.

I request immediate inspection and action against the polluting unit.

Enclosures: Photographs, water test report, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]
[Mobile Number]

problem_description: Illegal extraction of sand from rivers, riverbeds, or public land is causing environmental damage, flooding risk, and destruction of natural resources.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Document the illegal sand mining activity with photographs and video.
Step 2 – Note the location, time of mining, vehicles involved (registration numbers), and persons involved.
Step 3 – File a complaint with the Tehsildar or District Mines Department.
Step 4 – File a complaint with the local police station.
Step 5 – Report to the District Collector who is the Mining Officer.
Step 6 – File a complaint with the State Pollution Control Board for environmental damage.
Step 7 – File a PIL in the High Court for judicial intervention if official machinery is non-responsive.
escalation_path: Tehsildar → District Collector (Mining) → State Mines Department → State PCB → High Court PIL
complaint_template:

I request immediate action to stop the illegal mining and take legal action against those responsible.

Enclosures: Photographs, identity proof, vehicle numbers if known

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]
[Mobile Number]

subcategory: Open Defecation / Lack of Sanitation Complaint
problem_description: A community is suffering due to lack of proper sanitation facilities, open defecation near residential areas, or non-functioning public toilets.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Document the problem with photographs and note the locations of open defecation or non-functional facilities.
Step 2 – File a complaint with the local Gram Panchayat or Municipal Corporation.
Step 3 – Report to the Block Development Officer for rural areas or Urban Local Body for cities.
Step 4 – File a complaint on the Swachh Bharat Mission portal.
Step 5 – File a complaint with the District Collector about sanitation failure.
Step 6 – File a complaint on CPGRAMS for central government attention.
required_documents: Written complaint to Gram Panchayat / Municipal Corporation
escalation_path: Gram Panchayat → BDO → District Collector → State Sanitation Mission → CPGRAMS
complaint_template:
To,
The Sarpanch / Municipal Councillor,
[Gram Panchayat / Ward Name]

Subject: Complaint regarding lack of sanitation and open defecation

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], residing in [Village/Area], wish to report that the area near [Location] is being used for open defecation due to lack of proper sanitation facilities / non-functional public toilets. This is creating serious health and hygiene problems for our community.

I request immediate action to provide adequate sanitation facilities and stop open defecation in our area.

Enclosures: Photographs, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]
[Mobile Number]

subcategory: Senior Citizen Property Dispute with Children
problem_description: An elderly parent is being deprived of their own property by their adult children or relatives who are pressuring them to transfer property or have illegally taken possession.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – The senior citizen should approach the District Senior Citizen Protection Cell.
Step 2 – File a complaint under the Maintenance and Welfare of Parents and Senior Citizens Act, 2007.
Step 3 – The Maintenance Tribunal for Senior Citizens (usually at the SDM level) can be approached.
Step 4 – File a complaint with the local police if criminal intimidation or fraud is involved.
Step 5 – If property has been transferred through undue influence or fraud, file a civil suit for cancellation of transfer.
Step 6 – Approach DLSA for free legal aid.
Step 7 – Contact the Helpline for Senior Citizens (14567) for immediate assistance.
required_documents: Evidence of intimidation or pressure (witnesses, messages)
Medical reports if physical harm occurred
escalation_path: Senior Citizen Helpline (14567) → Senior Citizen Protection Cell → Maintenance Tribunal → Civil Court → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Sub-Divisional Magistrate (Maintenance Tribunal),
[Sub-Division], [District]

Subject: Complaint under Maintenance and Welfare of Parents and Senior Citizens Act, 2007 regarding property dispute

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], aged [Age] years, am the owner of property at [Address]. My [children/relatives] [Names] have been pressuring me to transfer my property and have [describe situation]. I am being deprived of my own property and am in distress.

I request the Tribunal to intervene, protect my property rights, and take action against my [children/relatives] under the Senior Citizens Act.

Enclosures: Property documents, identity/age proof, evidence of dispute

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]
[Mobile Number]
online_portals: State Women and Child Welfare Portal (Elders Section)

problem_description: An elderly person is being financially exploited by family members, caretakers, or strangers who are misusing their savings, pension, or assets through fraud, undue influence, or theft.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – The senior citizen or a concerned family member should call the Senior Citizen Helpline (14567).
Step 2 – Collect evidence of financial exploitation: bank statements, unauthorized transactions, witness accounts.
Step 3 – File a police complaint under relevant IPC sections (fraud, criminal breach of trust).
Step 4 – Inform the senior citizen's bank to block unauthorized access to accounts.
Step 5 – File a complaint with the Senior Citizen Protection Cell.
Step 6 – Approach the Maintenance Tribunal to protect the senior citizen's assets.
Step 7 – File a civil suit to recover misappropriated property or funds.
Step 8 – Seek a restraining order against the exploiting party.
required_documents: Identity proof and age proof of senior citizen

authorities.name: Local Police Station / Senior Citizen Protection Cell

escalation_path: Senior Citizen Helpline → Police → Maintenance Tribunal → Civil Court → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Station House Officer,
[Police Station Name]

Subject: Complaint regarding financial exploitation of senior citizen

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Name of Complainant / Senior Citizen], am [Age] years old and wish to report that [Exploiting Person(s)] have been financially exploiting me by [describe: withdrawing from bank account / misusing Power of Attorney / pressuring to transfer property / stealing funds].

I request immediate investigation and action to stop this exploitation and recover my funds/property.

Enclosures: Bank statements, identity proof, evidence of exploitation

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]
[Mobile Number]

problem_description: An elderly parent is being denied financial maintenance by their adult children despite having legal entitlement under the Maintenance and Welfare of Parents and Senior Citizens Act, 2007.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Call the Senior Citizen Helpline (14567) to register a complaint and receive guidance.
Step 2 – File a written application before the Maintenance Tribunal (SDM) under the Senior Citizens Act for maintenance.
Step 3 – The Tribunal will call the children for a hearing and can order up to Rs. 10,000 per month as maintenance.
Step 4 – If the children disobey the Tribunal order, they can face imprisonment of up to 3 months.
Step 5 – Also file under Section 125 CrPC in Magistrate Court for maintenance.
Step 6 – Contact DLSA for free legal representation.
Step 7 – Approach the State Appellate Tribunal if unsatisfied with Maintenance Tribunal order.
required_documents: Proof of parenthood (birth certificates of children, school records)
Evidence that children have refused maintenance
escalation_path: Senior Citizen Helpline → Maintenance Tribunal → Magistrate Court → State Appellate Tribunal
complaint_template:
To,
The Maintenance Tribunal (Sub-Divisional Magistrate),
[Sub-Division], [District]

Subject: Application for maintenance under the Maintenance and Welfare of Parents and Senior Citizens Act, 2007

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], aged [Age] years, am the parent of [Children's Names] who are employed and financially capable but have refused to provide for my maintenance. I am unable to meet my daily expenses and medical needs without their support.

I request the Tribunal to direct my children to pay monthly maintenance of Rs. [Amount] as per the Senior Citizens Act.

Enclosures: Identity/age proof, proof of parenthood, evidence of refusal

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]
[Mobile Number]

subcategory: Elderly Abuse by Caretaker / Domestic Worker
problem_description: An elderly person is being physically, emotionally, or financially abused by a hired caretaker, domestic worker, or paid helper.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Remove the senior citizen from the abusive situation immediately if safe to do so.
Step 2 – Seek medical attention and document injuries.
Step 3 – Call the Senior Citizen Helpline (14567) for immediate assistance.
Step 4 – File a police FIR against the abusive caretaker for assault, theft, or abuse.
Step 5 – File a complaint with the Senior Citizen Protection Cell.
Step 6 – Contact the District Collector if immediate protection is needed.
Step 7 – Arrange for alternative safe accommodation.
escalation_path: Senior Citizen Helpline → Police → Senior Citizen Protection Cell → District Collector
complaint_template:
To,
The Station House Officer,
[Police Station Name]

Subject: FIR complaint regarding abuse of senior citizen by caretaker

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name / Family Member], am writing to report that my [relation] [Senior Citizen's Name], aged [Age], has been physically/financially abused by their caretaker [Caretaker Name] who was employed at [Address].

The abuse includes [describe briefly]. I request immediate police action to arrest the abusive caretaker and protect the senior citizen.

Enclosures: Medical report, identity proof, employment record of caretaker

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]
[Mobile Number]

subcategory: Senior Citizen Discrimination in Public Services
problem_description: An elderly person is being denied priority services, reserved seating, pension, or other benefits they are legally entitled to in banks, hospitals, government offices, or public transport.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Note the specific denial and the institution/officer responsible.
Step 2 – File a written complaint with the head of the concerned institution.
Step 3 – Call the Senior Citizen Helpline (14567) and report the discrimination.
Step 4 – File a complaint with the District Social Welfare Officer.
Step 5 – File on CPGRAMS for government service failures.
Step 6 – Approach the State Vigilance Commissioner if corruption is involved.
Step 7 – File a complaint with the National Commission for Senior Citizens if established in your state.
required_documents: Senior Citizen ID card / Aadhaar showing age
Evidence of denial (written refusal or witness account)
escalation_path: Institution Head → District Social Welfare → State Department → CPGRAMS → National Level
complaint_template:
To,
The Head of Institution / District Social Welfare Officer,
[Institution/District]

Subject: Complaint regarding denial of senior citizen entitlements

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], aged [Age] years, wish to report that I was denied [priority service / reserved seating / pension / other entitlement] at [Institution Name] on [Date]. As a senior citizen, I am legally entitled to these benefits.

I request immediate action to ensure I receive my entitled benefits and that staff is trained to respect senior citizens' rights.

Enclosures: Senior Citizen ID, written complaint

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]
[Mobile Number]

subcategory: Senior Citizen Facing Medical Negligence / Denial of Treatment
problem_description: An elderly person has been denied medical treatment, has faced medical negligence, or is unable to access government health entitlements like Ayushman Bharat.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Call the Senior Citizen Helpline (14567) for medical assistance guidance.
Step 2 – File a complaint with the Hospital Superintendent or Medical Director.
Step 3 – Contact the State Health Department if a government hospital is involved.
Step 4 – File a complaint with the State Medical Council for negligence by doctors.
Step 5 – File a consumer complaint in the District Consumer Commission.
Step 6 – File a complaint with Ayushman Bharat helpline if scheme benefits are denied.
Step 7 – Approach NHRC if the right to health/life is being violated.
escalation_path: Hospital Administration → State Health Department → State Medical Council → Consumer Commission → NHRC
complaint_template:
To,
The Medical Superintendent,
[Hospital Name]

Subject: Complaint regarding denial of medical treatment to senior citizen

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name / Relative's Name], aged [Age] years, was denied [medical treatment / emergency care / Ayushman Bharat scheme benefit] at your hospital on [Date]. The denial caused [describe impact].

As a senior citizen, I have the right to medical care and I request an immediate explanation and provision of necessary treatment.

Enclosures: Medical records, Ayushman Bharat card, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]
[Mobile Number]

problem_description: A citizen's access road or traditional pathway to their land is being blocked by a neighbour or landowner, denying them the right of way they have used for years.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Collect evidence of the traditional pathway or right of way: old photographs, survey maps, and witness statements.
Step 2 – Check revenue records and land survey maps for any recorded right of way.
Step 3 – Send a written notice to the blocking party asserting your right of way.
Step 4 – File a complaint with the Tehsildar to verify if right of way is recorded in revenue records.
Step 5 – File a civil suit in Civil Court for declaration and enforcement of easement rights under the Easements Act, 1882.
Step 6 – Apply for an interim injunction to restore access while the case is pending.
Step 7 – Contact DLSA for free legal aid.
required_documents: Written notice sent to blocking party
escalation_path: Tehsildar → Civil Court → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Civil Court,
[District]

Subject: Suit for declaration and enforcement of right of way

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], own land bearing Survey No. [Survey No.] in village [Village Name]. My access to this land through the traditional pathway has been blocked by [Neighbour's Name] since [Date]. This pathway has been used by my family for over [Years] years and constitutes an easement right.

I request the court to declare my right of way and direct removal of the obstruction.

Enclosures: Survey map, 7/12 extract, witness statements, photographs

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

problem_description: A farmer's traditional right to use an irrigation channel or water source is being denied or diverted by a neighbouring farmer or landowner.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Gather old revenue records and irrigation maps showing your water rights.
Step 2 – Collect witness statements from other farmers who know about your traditional water rights.
Step 3 – File a complaint with the Gram Panchayat and request mediation.
Step 4 – Report to the Irrigation Department or Water Resources Department.
Step 5 – File a complaint with the Tehsildar for a revenue inquiry.
Step 6 – Approach the Civil Court for a declaration of water rights.
Step 7 – Contact DLSA for free legal representation.
escalation_path: Gram Panchayat → Tehsildar → Irrigation Department → Civil Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Tehsildar,
[Taluka], [District]

Subject: Complaint regarding denial of irrigation water rights

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], am a farmer owning land bearing Survey No. [Survey No.] in village [Village Name]. My traditional right to draw water from [Canal/Well/Source Name] has been illegally blocked by [Neighbour's Name] since [Date], causing damage to my standing crops.

I request an urgent revenue inquiry and restoration of my water rights.

Enclosures: 7/12 extract, revenue records, witness statements, photographs

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

subcategory: बिना मुआवजे के सरकार द्वारा भूमि का अनुचित कब्जा
problem_description: Government officials have seized or taken possession of a citizen's private land without issuing a formal acquisition notice or providing compensation.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Verify your ownership documents and confirm that no acquisition notice was received.
Step 2 – Visit the Tehsildar and District Collector's office to demand an explanation.
Step 3 – Send a legal notice to the government authority demanding return of land or compensation.
Step 4 – File a complaint with the District Collector against the unauthorized seizure.
Step 5 – File a writ petition in the High Court for violation of Article 300A of the Constitution (right to property).
Step 6 – Approach DLSA for free legal aid.
Step 7 – File a complaint with the National Human Rights Commission if rights are systematically violated.
escalation_path: District Collector → High Court (Writ Petition) → Supreme Court
complaint_template:
To,
The District Collector,
[District]

Subject: Complaint regarding unauthorized seizure of private land by government

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], am the owner of land bearing Survey No. [Survey No.] in village [Village Name]. Without any formal acquisition notice or compensation, government officials have seized/occupied my land since [Date].

This is a violation of my constitutional right to property under Article 300A. I request immediate release of my land or initiation of proper acquisition proceedings with fair compensation.

Enclosures: Ownership documents, 7/12 extract, photographs

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

problem_description: A builder or real estate developer has illegally occupied or grabbed private land for construction purposes using forged documents, political influence, or muscle power.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Collect all ownership documents and verify current land record status.
Step 2 – Photograph and document the illegal construction or occupation.
Step 3 – File an FIR at the local police station for trespass and land grabbing.
Step 4 – File a complaint with the District Collector and Revenue Department.
Step 5 – Approach the Sub-Divisional Magistrate for an emergency order to stop construction.
Step 6 – File a civil suit for injunction and recovery of possession.
Step 7 – File a PIL in the High Court if the builder has political protection.
escalation_path: Police FIR → SDM → District Collector → Civil Court → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Sub-Divisional Magistrate,
[Sub-Division], [District]

Subject: Complaint regarding illegal land grabbing by builder

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], am the owner of land bearing Survey No. [Survey No.] in [Village/Area]. The builder/developer [Name] has illegally occupied and commenced construction on my land using forged documents / without my consent.

I request your immediate intervention to stop the illegal construction and restore my land.

Enclosures: Ownership documents, photographs, FIR copy

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

problem_description: Private individuals have illegally occupied government land, village common land (Gairan/Shart), or public spaces like roads and parks.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Verify from revenue records that the land is indeed government or common land.
Step 2 – Photograph the encroachment with date stamps.
Step 3 – File a complaint with the Gram Panchayat / Municipal Corporation.
Step 4 – Report to the Tehsildar for revenue inquiry and encroachment removal.
Step 5 – If the Tehsildar does not act, escalate to the Sub-Divisional Magistrate.
Step 6 – File a complaint with the District Collector.
Step 7 – File a PIL in the High Court if large-scale encroachment is ignored by authorities.
required_documents: Revenue records showing government/common land classification
escalation_path: Gram Panchayat → Tehsildar → SDM → District Collector → High Court PIL
complaint_template:
To,
The Tehsildar,
[Taluka], [District]

Subject: Complaint regarding encroachment on government/common land

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], wish to report that government/common land bearing Survey No. [Survey No.] in village [Village Name] has been illegally encroached upon by [Name/Unknown Party]. This land belongs to the village community and should be free from private occupation.

I request your authority to conduct an inquiry and remove the encroachment at the earliest.

Enclosures: Revenue records, photographs, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

subcategory: बंधक धोखाधड़ी / बिना सहमति के संपत्ति गिरवी
problem_description: A citizen's property has been illegally mortgaged by a family member, agent, or fraudster without their knowledge or consent to obtain a loan.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Obtain a certified copy of the mortgage deed from the Sub-Registrar office.
Step 2 – Verify the mortgage details and identify who mortgaged the property.
Step 3 – File an FIR for fraud and forgery at the local police station.
Step 4 – Send a notice to the bank/lender informing them of the unauthorized mortgage.
Step 5 – File a civil suit for cancellation of the fraudulent mortgage deed.
Step 6 – File a complaint with the Banking Ombudsman if a bank is involved.
Step 7 – Approach the High Court if urgent relief is needed.
required_documents: Certified copy of fraudulent mortgage deed
escalation_path: Police FIR → Sub-Registrar → Civil Court → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Station House Officer,
[Police Station Name]

Subject: FIR complaint regarding fraudulent mortgage of property

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], am the owner of property at [Address]. I have discovered that my property has been fraudulently mortgaged to [Bank/Person] by [Fraudster Name] using forged documents without my knowledge or consent.

I request immediate FIR registration and investigation into this fraud.

Enclosures: Ownership documents, mortgage deed copy, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

subcategory: अनुमोदित लेआउट / हाउसिंग सोसाइटी में प्लॉट विवाद
problem_description: A buyer has purchased a plot in an approved layout or housing society but is facing issues like non-delivery of possession, boundary disputes with neighbours, or developer not providing promised amenities.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Collect the sale deed, approved layout plan, and all payment receipts.
Step 2 – Verify your plot number and boundaries against the approved layout plan.
Step 3 – Write to the housing society or developer for resolution.
Step 4 – File a complaint with RERA for developer-related issues.
Step 5 – File a consumer complaint in the District Consumer Commission.
Step 6 – File a complaint with the Registrar of Co-operative Societies for housing society disputes.
Step 7 – Approach the Civil Court for boundary or title disputes.
escalation_path: RERA → Consumer Commission → Civil Court → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Real Estate Regulatory Authority,
[State]

Subject: Complaint regarding plot in approved housing layout

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], purchased Plot No. [Plot No.] in [Layout Name] from [Developer Name] and paid Rs. [Amount]. The developer has not delivered possession / is not providing promised amenities / has changed the layout without permission.

I request RERA to direct the developer to deliver my plot or refund with interest.

Enclosures: Sale deed, layout plan, payment receipts

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

subcategory: मौखिक उपहार या वसीयत से उत्पन्न विवाद
problem_description: A family dispute arises when one member claims property was gifted or willed to them orally by a deceased relative, while other heirs dispute this claim.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Gather all available evidence of the oral gift or will: witness statements, written notes.
Step 2 – Obtain a copy of any registered will from the Sub-Registrar office.
Step 3 – Consult a lawyer about the legal status of oral gifts and wills under applicable law.
Step 4 – Attempt mediation through the District Legal Services Authority.
Step 5 – File a civil suit for declaration of ownership in Civil Court.
Step 6 – Obtain a succession certificate or letters of administration from the court if needed.
Step 7 – The court will evaluate evidence from all parties and determine valid ownership.
required_documents: Written notes or letters about the gift/will
escalation_path: DLSA Mediation → Civil Court → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Civil Court,
[District]

Subject: Suit for declaration of ownership based on gift/will

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], claim ownership of property bearing Survey No. [Survey No.] based on an oral gift/will made by the late [Name] in my favour. The other heirs are disputing this claim.

I request the court to declare my ownership of the said property based on the evidence I will present.

Enclosures: Property documents, witness statements, death certificate

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

subcategory: खरीद के बाद संपत्ति का गैर-पंजीकरण
problem_description: A buyer has paid for a property but the seller is refusing to execute the registered sale deed, leaving the buyer with no legal title to the property.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Serve a legal notice to the seller demanding execution of the registered sale deed within 30 days.
Step 2 – File a suit for specific performance of the sale agreement in Civil Court.
Step 3 – The court will direct the seller to execute and register the sale deed.
Step 4 – If the seller has already sold the property to someone else, file a fraud complaint with police.
Step 5 – Apply for an interim injunction to prevent the seller from further dealing with the property.
Step 6 – Approach DLSA for free legal representation.
escalation_path: Legal Notice → Civil Court (Specific Performance) → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Civil Court,
[District]

Subject: Suit for specific performance of property sale agreement

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], entered into a sale agreement dated [Date] with [Seller Name] for property at [Address] for a total consideration of Rs. [Amount]. I have paid the full amount / Rs. [Amount] as per the agreement. Despite repeated requests, the seller has refused to execute the registered sale deed.

I request the court to direct the seller to execute and register the sale deed in my favour.

Enclosures: Sale agreement, payment receipts, legal notice

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

subcategory: कृषि किरायेदार के किरायेदारी अधिकार (खानदानी/स्थायी किरायेदारी)
problem_description: An agricultural tenant who has been cultivating land for many years is being illegally dispossessed by the landowner without following the procedure under the applicable Tenancy Act.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Collect evidence of long-term cultivation: old 7/12 entries in your name, receipts of rent paid.
Step 2 – Check the applicable state Tenancy Act for your rights as a protected tenant.
Step 3 – File a complaint with the Tehsildar stating you are a protected tenant.
Step 4 – The Tehsildar will conduct an inquiry under the Tenancy Act.
Step 5 – If dispossession is threatened, apply for stay of eviction to the Tehsildar or SDM.
Step 6 – Approach the Revenue Tribunal if the Tehsildar order is against you.
Step 7 – Seek free legal aid from DLSA.
required_documents: 7/12 extracts showing your name as cultivator
escalation_path: Tehsildar → SDM → Revenue Tribunal → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Tehsildar,
[Taluka], [District]

Subject: Complaint regarding illegal dispossession of protected agricultural tenant

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], have been cultivating land bearing Survey No. [Survey No.] owned by [Landowner Name] for over [Years] years as a protected tenant. The landowner is now attempting to illegally dispossess me without following the procedure under the Tenancy Act.

I request your intervention to protect my tenancy rights and prevent illegal eviction.

Enclosures: 7/12 extracts, rent receipts, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

problem_description: An employee who has completed 5 or more years of continuous service is denied gratuity payment by the employer after leaving the job, as required under the Payment of Gratuity Act, 1972.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Calculate your gratuity entitlement (15 days salary × years of service).
Step 2 – Send a written application to the employer for gratuity payment.
Step 3 – If not paid within 30 days, file a complaint with the Controlling Authority under the Gratuity Act (Labour Commissioner).
Step 4 – The Controlling Authority will issue a notice to the employer and determine the gratuity amount.
Step 5 – If the employer still fails to pay, apply for a certificate of recovery.
Step 6 – File an appeal before the Appellate Authority if dissatisfied with the Controlling Authority's order.
Step 7 – Approach the Labour Court for enforcement of payment.
required_documents: Application for gratuity submitted to employer
escalation_path: Labour Commissioner → Appellate Authority → Labour Court → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Controlling Authority (Gratuity Act),
Labour Commissioner's Office,
[District]

Subject: Complaint regarding non-payment of gratuity

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], was employed at [Company Name] from [Date of Joining] to [Date of Leaving], completing [Years] years of continuous service. I applied for gratuity of Rs. [Amount] on [Date] but my employer has refused to pay it.

I request your authority to direct my employer to pay my gratuity with interest as per the Payment of Gratuity Act, 1972.

Enclosures: Appointment letter, service records, salary slips, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

problem_description: Casual or daily wage workers are being denied minimum wages, employment benefits, or regularisation despite years of continuous service with a government or private employer.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Collect evidence of continuous employment: wage slips, muster rolls, gate passes.
Step 2 – Check the applicable state minimum wage notification for your category of work.
Step 3 – File a complaint with the Labour Commissioner for non-payment of minimum wages.
Step 4 – Raise an industrial dispute through the Labour Commissioner's office.
Step 5 – For government casual workers, approach the Administrative Tribunal or High Court.
Step 6 – Approach DLSA for free legal aid in court proceedings.
required_documents: Gate pass or work entry records
escalation_path: Labour Commissioner → Labour Court → Administrative Tribunal → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Labour Commissioner,
[District]

Subject: Complaint regarding denial of rights to casual/daily wage worker

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], have been working as a casual/daily wage worker at [Employer Name] since [Date]. I am being paid below the minimum wage of Rs. [Applicable Rate] and denied statutory benefits despite [Years] of continuous service.

I request your office to investigate and ensure payment of minimum wages and applicable benefits.

Enclosures: Wage records, muster rolls, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

subcategory: ओवरटाइम मजदूरी का भुगतान न करना
problem_description: An employer is making workers work more than the legally mandated hours without paying overtime wages as required under the Factories Act or other labour laws.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Record the overtime hours worked with dates and duration.
Step 2 – Confirm entitlement to double wages for overtime under the Factories Act / Minimum Wages Act.
Step 3 – Send a written demand to the employer for overtime payment.
Step 4 – File a complaint with the Labour Commissioner or Factory Inspector.
Step 5 – File an application in Labour Court for recovery of overtime wages.
Step 6 – Contact DLSA for free legal assistance.
required_documents: Register of working hours or overtime records
escalation_path: Factory Inspector → Labour Commissioner → Labour Court → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Labour Commissioner,
[District]

Subject: Complaint regarding non-payment of overtime wages

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], am employed at [Company Name]. I regularly work [X] hours per day / [X] hours per week beyond the prescribed limit but am not paid overtime wages as required by law.

I request your office to direct my employer to pay all outstanding overtime wages.

Enclosures: Overtime records, salary slips, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

problem_description: An employer is making unauthorized deductions from an employee's salary for reasons not permitted under the Payment of Wages Act, such as fines, recovery of loans without agreement, or arbitrary deductions.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Identify the unauthorized deductions in your salary slips.
Step 2 – Send a written protest to your employer citing the Payment of Wages Act.
Step 3 – File a complaint with the Payment of Wages Authority (Labour Commissioner).
Step 4 – The Authority will investigate and direct refund of illegal deductions.
Step 5 – File a case in the Labour Court if the Authority is non-responsive.
Step 6 – Seek free legal aid from DLSA.

authorities.name: Payment of Wages Authority / Labour Commissioner

escalation_path: Labour Commissioner → Labour Court → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Payment of Wages Authority,
Labour Commissioner's Office,
[District]

Subject: Complaint regarding illegal deductions from salary

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], am employed at [Company Name]. My employer has been making unauthorized deductions of Rs. [Amount] per month from my salary for [reason given by employer] which is not permitted under the Payment of Wages Act, 1936.

I request your authority to investigate and direct my employer to refund all illegal deductions.

Enclosures: Salary slips, appointment letter, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

problem_description: A worker suffered an injury or a worker died in a workplace accident and the employer is refusing to pay compensation under the Employees' Compensation Act, 1923.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Seek immediate medical treatment and preserve all medical reports.
Step 2 – Report the accident to the employer and ensure it is recorded in the accident register.
Step 3 – File a claim for compensation with the Employees' Compensation Commissioner (Labour Commissioner).
Step 4 – The Commissioner will calculate compensation based on the nature of injury and wages.
Step 5 – If the employer disputes the claim, the Commissioner will conduct a formal hearing.
Step 6 – The Commissioner will pass an award directing the employer to pay compensation.
Step 7 – Appeal to the High Court if dissatisfied with the Commissioner's order.
escalation_path: Compensation Commissioner → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Employees' Compensation Commissioner,
Labour Commissioner's Office,
[District]

Subject: Claim for compensation under Employees' Compensation Act, 1923

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name / Name of deceased's family], was employed as [Designation] at [Company Name]. On [Date], I sustained injuries / [Name] died in a workplace accident at [Location]. My employer has refused to pay compensation.

I request the Commissioner to assess and award compensation as per the Employees' Compensation Act.

Enclosures: Medical reports, accident report, salary slips, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

problem_description: Workers are being prevented from forming or joining a trade union, or union leaders are being victimised, transferred, or terminated for union activities.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Document all incidents of victimisation or union suppression.
Step 2 – Approach the Trade Union office or national federation for guidance.
Step 3 – File a complaint with the Labour Commissioner for unfair labour practice.
Step 4 – Raise an industrial dispute under the Industrial Disputes Act, 1947.
Step 5 – File a complaint with the Industrial Tribunal.
Step 6 – Approach the High Court for protection of fundamental right to form associations (Article 19).
required_documents: Evidence of victimisation (transfer orders, termination letters)
escalation_path: Labour Commissioner → Industrial Tribunal → High Court → Supreme Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Labour Commissioner,
[District]

Subject: Complaint regarding unfair labour practice / suppression of trade union rights

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], am a worker and trade union leader at [Company Name]. Our management has been engaged in unfair labour practices by [describe: terminating union leaders / preventing workers from joining union / transferring union office bearers].

This violates the Industrial Disputes Act and our fundamental rights. I request your intervention to stop these unfair practices.

Enclosures: Evidence of victimisation, union registration, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

problem_description: Contract workers employed through a contractor at a principal employer's establishment are being denied wages, benefits, or regularisation despite working alongside permanent employees doing the same work.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Collect all evidence of your employment through the contractor and your actual work at the principal employer's premises.
Step 2 – Check if the principal employer is responsible for wages if the contractor defaults (under Contract Labour Act).
Step 3 – File a complaint with the Labour Commissioner against the contractor and principal employer.
Step 4 – Raise an industrial dispute for equal pay and regularisation.
Step 5 – Approach the Industrial Tribunal for regularisation if continuously employed for years.
Step 6 – File a writ petition in High Court for equal pay for equal work.
required_documents: Evidence of work at principal employer's premises
escalation_path: Labour Commissioner → Industrial Tribunal → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Labour Commissioner,
[District]

Subject: Complaint regarding exploitation of contract labour

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], have been working as a contract labour at [Principal Employer Name] through contractor [Contractor Name] since [Date]. I am performing the same work as permanent employees but am paid below minimum wage / denied benefits / facing non-payment of wages.

I request your office to investigate and ensure I receive my legal entitlements.

Enclosures: Wage slips, attendance records, appointment letter, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

problem_description: An employer is refusing to grant earned leave, sick leave, casual leave, or other legally mandated leave entitlements to employees.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Review your employment contract and company leave policy.
Step 2 – Check applicable state labour laws for your leave entitlements.
Step 3 – Submit a formal leave application in writing to HR/management.
Step 4 – If leave is denied without reason, file a complaint with the Labour Commissioner.
Step 5 – If leave encashment is denied on resignation, file a claim with Labour Commissioner.
Step 6 – Approach the Labour Court if administrative remedy fails.
Step 7 – Contact DLSA for free legal aid.
escalation_path: Labour Commissioner → Labour Court → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Labour Commissioner,
[District]

Subject: Complaint regarding denial of statutory leave entitlement

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], am employed at [Company Name]. Despite submitting a leave application on [Date] for [Type of Leave], my employer has denied my leave without valid reason. I am entitled to this leave under the [applicable Act].

I request your office to direct my employer to grant my statutory leave entitlement.

Enclosures: Leave application, denial letter, appointment letter, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

problem_description: Migrant workers from rural areas are being lured to cities or other states with false promises of wages and then exploited, denied wages, or prevented from returning home.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – If in immediate danger, call Police Emergency (112) or Childline (1098 if minor).
Step 2 – Contact the Labour Commissioner in the destination state.
Step 3 – File a complaint with the local police under the Inter-State Migrant Workmen Act.
Step 4 – Contact the Anti-Human Trafficking Unit of the police.
Step 5 – Report to the National Human Rights Commission.
Step 6 – Contact NGOs that assist migrant workers for rescue and repatriation.
Step 7 – File a complaint with the Labour Commissioner in the home state for follow-up.
required_documents: Any employment agreement or offer letter
Details of contractor who brought the worker
escalation_path: Police → Anti-Trafficking Unit → Labour Commissioner → NHRC
complaint_template:
To,
The Station House Officer / Anti-Human Trafficking Unit,
[Police Station]

Subject: Complaint regarding exploitation of migrant workers

Respected Sir/Madam,

I / [Name of victim] came from [Home State] to [Current Location] on the promise of employment by [Contractor Name]. We have been exploited by [describe: not paid wages / prevented from leaving / kept in bondage / working in dangerous conditions].

I request immediate police intervention and repatriation assistance.

Enclosures: Identity proof, any employment documents

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

problem_description: A citizen was lured into a fake investment scheme, fraudulent stock tips, or a Ponzi scheme through social media, messaging apps, or phone calls, resulting in financial loss.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Stop sending any more money to the fraudster immediately.
Step 2 – Collect all evidence: screenshots, transaction records, communication with fraudster.
Step 3 – Call the Cyber Fraud Helpline (1930) to report and attempt to freeze the fraudster's account.
Step 4 – File a complaint on the National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal (cybercrime.gov.in).
Step 5 – File a complaint with SEBI (Securities and Exchange Board of India) if stock market fraud is involved.
Step 6 – File an FIR at the Cyber Crime Police Station.
Step 7 – File a complaint with the RBI if the scheme involved unregulated financial activity.
Step 8 – Approach the Economic Offences Wing of the police for large-scale fraud.
required_documents: Screenshots of investment platform and communication
Details of fraudster (name, bank account, phone)
Any investment documents or certificates given
escalation_path: Cyber Fraud Helpline → Cyber Crime Police → SEBI/RBI → Economic Offences Wing → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Station House Officer,
Cyber Crime Police Station

Subject: Complaint regarding investment/stock market fraud

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], was approached by [Name/Group] through [Platform] with an investment scheme promising [returns]. I invested Rs. [Amount] through transactions on [Dates]. The fraudsters have now stopped responding and my invested amount is not being returned.

I request immediate investigation and recovery of my invested amount.

Enclosures: Bank statements, screenshots, communication records, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]
[Mobile Number]

problem_description: A citizen was deceived by a fraudulent email or fake website mimicking a bank, government portal, or e-commerce site, leading to credential theft or financial loss.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Do not click any further links and close the fake website.
Step 2 – Change your passwords immediately for all affected accounts.
Step 3 – Call your bank to block the card and reverse unauthorized transactions.
Step 4 – Take screenshots of the phishing email or fake website URL.
Step 5 – File a complaint on the National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal.
Step 6 – Report the phishing website to CERT-In (India's cyber emergency body).
Step 7 – File an FIR at the Cyber Crime Police Station.
Step 8 – File a complaint with the Banking Ombudsman for unauthorized transactions.
required_documents: Screenshot of phishing email with headers
escalation_path: Cyber Fraud Helpline → CERT-In → Cyber Crime Police → Banking Ombudsman
complaint_template:
To,
The Station House Officer,
Cyber Crime Police Station

Subject: Complaint regarding phishing fraud

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], received a phishing email/message on [Date] purportedly from [Bank/Institution Name] directing me to a fake website. I entered my credentials on the fake site and subsequently Rs. [Amount] was fraudulently debited from my account.

I request investigation and recovery of the fraudulent amount.

Enclosures: Screenshots of email and fake website, bank statement, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]
[Mobile Number]

problem_description: A fraudster obtained a duplicate SIM card of the victim's mobile number by submitting forged documents to the telecom operator, then used it to access bank accounts by receiving OTPs.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Notice if your mobile SIM suddenly stops working – this may indicate a SIM swap.
Step 2 – Immediately call your bank to block all transactions.
Step 3 – Visit your telecom operator's store and report the unauthorized SIM swap.
Step 4 – Call the Cyber Fraud Helpline (1930) to report the fraud.
Step 5 – File a police FIR against the fraudster and telecom operator employee (if complicit).
Step 6 – File a complaint on the National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal.
Step 7 – File a complaint with TRAI about the telecom operator's failure of KYC process.
Step 8 – File a Banking Ombudsman complaint for unauthorized transactions.
required_documents: Affidavit stating you did not apply for duplicate SIM
escalation_path: Bank → Telecom Operator → TRAI → Cyber Crime Police → Banking Ombudsman → RBI
complaint_template:
To,
The Station House Officer,
Cyber Crime Police Station

Subject: Complaint regarding SIM swap fraud

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], discovered on [Date] that my mobile number [Number] had been subject to an unauthorized SIM swap. A fraudster obtained a duplicate SIM and used it to access my bank account and transfer Rs. [Amount].

I request investigation and recovery of the fraudulently transferred amount.

Enclosures: Bank statement, telecom complaint reference, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]
[Mobile Number]

subcategory: व्यक्तिगत उपकरण पर रैनसमवेयर / मैलवेयर हमला
problem_description: A citizen's computer or mobile device has been infected with ransomware that has encrypted their files, or with malware that is stealing personal and financial data.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Disconnect the device from the internet immediately.
Step 2 – Do NOT pay the ransom demanded by attackers.
Step 3 – Report to CERT-In through their website or helpline.
Step 4 – File a complaint on the National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal.
Step 5 – File an FIR at the Cyber Crime Police Station.
Step 6 – Seek help from a certified cybersecurity professional to recover the device.
Step 7 – If financial data was stolen, immediately inform your bank and change all passwords.
required_documents: List of affected files or data
escalation_path: CERT-In → Cyber Crime Police → State Cyber Cell
complaint_template:
To,
The Station House Officer,
Cyber Crime Police Station

Subject: Complaint regarding ransomware/malware attack

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], discovered on [Date] that my computer/mobile device was attacked by ransomware/malware. My files have been encrypted and a ransom of [Amount/Currency] has been demanded. / My personal and financial data has been stolen.

I request investigation and technical assistance.

Enclosures: Screenshots of ransom demand, device details, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]
[Mobile Number]

problem_description: A person was cheated by someone on a matrimonial website who created a fake profile to obtain money, gifts, or personal information under false pretense of marriage.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Stop all communication with the suspected fraudster.
Step 2 – Save all messages, profiles, transaction records, and communication.
Step 3 – Report the fake profile to the matrimonial platform.
Step 4 – File a complaint on the National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal.
Step 5 – File an FIR at the Cyber Crime Police Station for cheating and fraud.
Step 6 – If money was transferred, file a complaint with Cyber Fraud Helpline (1930) immediately.
Step 7 – Contact National Commission for Women if gender-based fraud and exploitation occurred.
required_documents: Payment receipts if money was sent
escalation_path: Cyber Fraud Helpline → Cyber Crime Portal → Cyber Crime Police → NCW
complaint_template:
To,
The Station House Officer,
Cyber Crime Police Station

Subject: Complaint regarding matrimonial fraud

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], was contacted through [Matrimonial Platform] by [Fraudster's Name/Profile] who falsely posed as a potential marriage partner. Over [Time Period], I was deceived into sending Rs. [Amount] / sharing personal information. The person has now disappeared.

I request investigation and action against the fraudster.

Enclosures: Profile screenshots, communication records, payment receipts, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]
[Mobile Number]

problem_description: A citizen paid money on a fake website that mimicked a government portal (driving licence, passport, PAN card services) and received nothing.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Verify you were using an unofficial website and not the actual government portal.
Step 2 – Save screenshots of the fake website and all transaction details.
Step 3 – File a complaint on the National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal.
Step 4 – File a complaint with Cyber Fraud Helpline (1930).
Step 5 – File an FIR at the Cyber Crime Police Station.
Step 6 – Report the fake website to CERT-In for takedown.
Step 7 – File a complaint with CPGRAMS if a government service was misrepresented.
escalation_path: Cyber Fraud Helpline → CERT-In → Cyber Crime Police → CPGRAMS
complaint_template:
To,
The Station House Officer,
Cyber Crime Police Station

Subject: Complaint regarding fake government portal fraud

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], paid Rs. [Amount] on [Date] to a website [URL] believing it to be the official [Government Service] portal. After payment, no service was provided and the website appears to be fake.

I request investigation and recovery of my payment.

Enclosures: Screenshot of fake website, payment receipt, bank statement, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]
[Mobile Number]

subcategory: डेटा गोपनीयता उल्लंघन / व्यक्तिगत डेटा लीक
problem_description: A citizen's personal data (Aadhaar, PAN, bank details, photographs) has been leaked or sold by a company, app, or individual without consent.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Identify how you discovered the data breach (dark web alerts, spam calls using personal details).
Step 2 – File a complaint with the company/platform that leaked your data.
Step 3 – File a complaint with the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY).
Step 4 – File a complaint with CERT-In about the data breach.
Step 5 – Lock your Aadhaar biometric on UIDAI website.
Step 6 – File an FIR at the Cyber Crime Police Station.
Step 7 – Once the Personal Data Protection Act is fully operational, file a complaint with the Data Protection Board.
required_documents: Evidence of data breach (spam messages using your data, dark web listing)
Account details with the platform that leaked data
escalation_path: CERT-In → MeitY → Cyber Crime Police → Data Protection Board
complaint_template:
To,
The Director General,
CERT-In

Subject: Complaint regarding personal data breach

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], have discovered that my personal data including [describe: Aadhaar details / bank information / photographs] has been leaked / sold by [Company/Platform Name]. Evidence includes [describe how you found out].

I request investigation into the breach and action against the responsible party.

Enclosures: Evidence of breach, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]
[Mobile Number]

problem_description: A citizen lost money through a fraudulent online gaming app, fantasy sports platform, or in-app purchases that charged real money without proper disclosure.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Collect all payment receipts, app screenshots, and transaction records.
Step 2 – File a consumer complaint on the National Consumer Helpline (1915).
Step 3 – File a complaint on the National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal if fraud is involved.
Step 4 – Report the app to Google Play Store or Apple App Store.
Step 5 – File a case in the Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission.
Step 6 – File an FIR at the Cyber Crime Police Station if the platform was fraudulent.
escalation_path: Consumer Helpline → Cyber Crime Portal → Consumer Commission → Police FIR
complaint_template:
To,
The District Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission,
[District]

Subject: Consumer complaint regarding online gaming/app fraud

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], was using [App Name] which charged Rs. [Amount] without proper disclosure / used deceptive practices / was a fraudulent platform. I lost Rs. [Amount] through [describe circumstances].

I request the commission to direct the platform to refund Rs. [Amount] with compensation.

Enclosures: App screenshots, payment records, bank statements

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

problem_description: A consumer purchased a new or used vehicle that has manufacturing defects, and the dealer or manufacturer is refusing to repair, replace, or refund.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Document all defects with photographs and written descriptions.
Step 2 – Report the defect to the authorised dealer and request repair under warranty.
Step 3 – If the dealer cannot repair after multiple attempts, send a legal notice to the manufacturer.
Step 4 – File a complaint with the National Consumer Helpline (1915).
Step 5 – File a consumer case in the District Consumer Commission for replacement or refund.
Step 6 – Report to SIAM (Society of Indian Automobile Manufacturers) or the Ministry of Road Transport.
Step 7 – File a complaint on CPGRAMS if the vehicle is from a public sector unit.

authorities.name: Ministry of Road Transport and Highways

escalation_path: Dealer → National Consumer Helpline → Consumer Commission → State Commission
complaint_template:
To,
The President,
District Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission,
[District]

Subject: Consumer complaint regarding manufacturing defect in vehicle

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], purchased a [Vehicle Make/Model] (Registration No. [Number]) from [Dealer Name] on [Date] for Rs. [Amount]. The vehicle developed [describe defects] immediately after purchase. Despite [Number] visits to the service centre, the defect has not been resolved.

I request the commission to direct the manufacturer/dealer to replace the vehicle / refund Rs. [Amount] with compensation.

Enclosures: Invoice, warranty card, job cards, photographs, legal notice

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

subcategory: गैस / एलपीजी सिलेंडर अधिक शुल्क या गैर-डिलीवरी
problem_description: A consumer is being overcharged for LPG cylinders, denied subsidy, or the gas distributor is not delivering cylinders on time or supplying them to black market.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Note the overcharge amount and verify the correct MRP from the oil company's website.
Step 2 – File a complaint with the LPG distributor in writing.
Step 3 – File a complaint on the oil company's customer helpline.
Step 4 – Report to the Ministry of Petroleum's customer portal.
Step 5 – File a consumer complaint in the District Consumer Commission.
Step 6 – File a complaint with the District Supply Officer.
Step 7 – File on CPGRAMS if the issue persists.
escalation_path: Distributor → Oil Company → District Supply Officer → Consumer Commission → CPGRAMS
complaint_template:
To,
The Customer Care,
[Oil Company Name – IOCL/HPCL/BPCL]

Subject: Complaint regarding LPG overcharging / non-delivery

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], am an LPG consumer (Consumer No. [Number]) with distributor [Name]. I was charged Rs. [Amount] instead of the MRP of Rs. [Amount] / my cylinder has not been delivered for [Days] despite booking on [Date].

I request immediate resolution and action against the distributor.

Enclosures: Consumer card, delivery receipt, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]
[Mobile Number]

problem_description: A consumer purchased adulterated, substandard, or falsely labelled food products and suffered illness or was cheated about the contents.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Preserve the food product packaging and remaining contents as evidence.
Step 2 – Seek medical treatment if ill and get a medical report.
Step 3 – File a complaint with the Food Safety and Standards Authority of India (FSSAI) or the local food safety officer.
Step 4 – File a complaint with the National Consumer Helpline (1915).
Step 5 – File a consumer case in the District Consumer Commission.
Step 6 – File a police FIR if illness was severe or death occurred.
Step 7 – Report to the District Collector if a public health emergency is involved.
escalation_path: FSSAI → Consumer Commission → Police FIR → District Collector
complaint_template:
To,
The Designated Food Safety Officer,
[District]

Subject: Complaint regarding adulterated/substandard food product

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], purchased [Product Name / Brand] from [Shop Name] on [Date] for Rs. [Amount]. The product was [describe: adulterated / expired / falsely labelled / caused illness]. I have preserved the packaging and remaining product.

I request inspection and action against the manufacturer/retailer under the FSSAI Act.

Enclosures: Product packaging, purchase receipt, medical report, photographs

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

subcategory: जल आपूर्ति शिकायत / दूषित पाइप जल
problem_description: Citizens are receiving no water or contaminated water supply through municipal or government pipes, affecting their health and daily life.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Collect water samples in clean bottles as evidence.
Step 2 – Get the water tested at a government-approved laboratory.
Step 3 – File a complaint with the local Municipal Corporation Water Department.
Step 4 – File a complaint with the District Collector if the problem is widespread.
Step 5 – File a complaint with the State Water Supply and Sanitation Department.
Step 6 – File on CPGRAMS.
Step 7 – Approach the National Green Tribunal or High Court for systemic water quality issues.
escalation_path: Municipal Corporation → District Collector → State Water Department → NGT → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Executive Engineer (Water Supply),
[Municipal Corporation]

Subject: Complaint regarding contaminated / no piped water supply

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], residing at [Address], wish to report that the piped water supply in our area has been contaminated / irregular / absent since [Date]. Water test reports show [describe contamination]. This is causing health problems for our community.

I request immediate repair of the water supply system and provision of clean water.

Enclosures: Water test report, photographs, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

subcategory: ऑनलाइन शिक्षा मंच धोखाधड़ी / धनवापसी विवाद
problem_description: A student paid fees to an online coaching platform, ed-tech company, or course provider that did not deliver promised courses, cancelled courses, or refused to provide refunds.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Collect all fee receipts, course enrolment confirmation, and terms and conditions.
Step 2 – Send a written refund request to the platform citing their refund policy.
Step 3 – File a complaint on the National Consumer Helpline (1915).
Step 4 – File a consumer case in the District Consumer Commission.
Step 5 – File a complaint with the Ministry of Education if the institution is regulated.
Step 6 – File a complaint on the Cyber Crime Portal if fraud is involved.
escalation_path: Platform Customer Care → Consumer Helpline → Consumer Commission → Ministry of Education
complaint_template:
To,
The District Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission,
[District]

Subject: Consumer complaint regarding ed-tech / online course refund dispute

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], enrolled in [Course Name] on [Platform Name] on [Date] and paid Rs. [Amount]. The platform [did not deliver the promised course / cancelled the course / provided substandard content] and has refused to refund my fees despite repeated requests.

I request the commission to direct the platform to refund Rs. [Amount] with compensation.

Enclosures: Fee receipt, enrolment confirmation, refund request, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

subcategory: कूरियर / लॉजिस्टिक्स सेवा कमी (खोया या क्षतिग्रस्त पैकेज)
problem_description: A courier or logistics company lost, damaged, or delayed a valuable package and is refusing to compensate the sender or recipient.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – File a complaint with the courier company's customer service immediately.
Step 2 – Get a written complaint acknowledgment from the courier company.
Step 3 – File a complaint with the National Consumer Helpline (1915).
Step 4 – File a consumer case in the District Consumer Commission.
Step 5 – If the item was insured, file an insurance claim with the insurer.
Step 6 – For postal department issues, file a complaint with the Postmaster General.
escalation_path: Courier Company → Consumer Helpline → Consumer Commission → Civil Court
complaint_template:
To,
The District Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission,
[District]

Subject: Consumer complaint regarding lost/damaged courier package

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], booked a courier shipment (Tracking No. [Number]) with [Courier Company] on [Date] containing [describe contents] valued at Rs. [Amount]. The package was lost / delivered damaged, and the company has refused to compensate me.

I request the commission to direct the courier company to pay compensation of Rs. [Amount].

Enclosures: Courier receipt, contents declaration, damage photographs, company complaint

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

problem_description: A citizen's home was broken into and valuables were stolen while the family was away or asleep.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Do not disturb the crime scene – preserve footprints, fingerprints.
Step 2 – Call Police Emergency (112) immediately.
Step 3 – File an FIR at the local police station with a full list of stolen items.
Step 4 – Request police to conduct a forensic examination of the crime scene.
Step 5 – File an insurance claim for stolen items using the FIR copy.
Step 6 – Inform neighbours and local community watch groups.
Step 7 – Follow up with the investigating officer regularly.
required_documents: List of stolen items with descriptions and values
escalation_path: Police Station → Superintendent of Police → CID → Magistrate Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Station House Officer,
[Police Station]

Subject: FIR complaint regarding house break-in and robbery

Respected Sir/Madam,
