I, [Your Name], residing at [Address], wish to report that my house was broken into on [Date] during [time approximate]. The following items were stolen: [list items with values]. The point of entry was [describe location].

I request FIR registration and investigation to recover the stolen property.

Enclosures: List of stolen items, photographs, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]
[Mobile Number]

problem_description: A citizen's vehicle (car, motorcycle, bicycle, auto-rickshaw) has been stolen from a public place, parking area, or residential colony.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Note the last location where the vehicle was parked and any witnesses.
Step 2 – File an FIR immediately at the local police station.
Step 3 – Provide the vehicle registration number, chassis number, engine number, and photographs.
Step 4 – Get the FIR copy and approach the RTO for a No-Objection Certificate if needed.
Step 5 – File an insurance claim using the FIR copy.
Step 6 – Check CCTV cameras near the area with police assistance.
Step 7 – Follow up regularly with the investigating officer.
escalation_path: Police Station → SP → State Crime Branch → Interpol (if taken out of state)
complaint_template:
To,
The Station House Officer,
[Police Station]

Subject: FIR complaint regarding vehicle theft

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], wish to report that my [Vehicle Type] (Registration No. [Number], Chassis No. [Number], Engine No. [Number]) was stolen from [Location] on [Date] between [Time Range].

I request FIR registration and immediate action to trace and recover my vehicle.

Enclosures: RC copy, insurance, vehicle photographs, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]
[Mobile Number]

problem_description: A family member suspects that the death of their relative was not natural and was a murder or suspicious death, and the police are not registering a case or investigating properly.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Ensure that a post-mortem (autopsy) examination is conducted by a government doctor.
Step 2 – File a complaint at the local police station for investigation of suspicious death.
Step 3 – If police refuse to investigate, file a complaint with the Superintendent of Police.
Step 4 – File a complaint with the Judicial Magistrate under Section 174/176 CrPC for inquest.
Step 5 – Approach the High Court with a writ petition demanding proper investigation.
Step 6 – File a complaint with the National Human Rights Commission if police are not acting.
Step 7 – Seek free legal aid from DLSA.
required_documents: Identity proof of complainant and deceased
escalation_path: Police Station → SP → Magistrate → High Court → NHRC
complaint_template:
To,
The Superintendent of Police,
[District]

Subject: Complaint regarding suspicious death / failure to investigate murder

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], am the [relation] of [Deceased Name] who died on [Date] under suspicious circumstances. Despite my complaint to [Police Station Name], proper investigation has not been initiated.

I request your immediate intervention to direct a proper investigation into this suspicious death.

Enclosures: Post-mortem report, death certificate, written complaint, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]
[Mobile Number]

subcategory: अवैध हथियार / शस्त्र रखने की शिकायत
problem_description: A citizen is aware that a person in their area is illegally possessing firearms, sharp weapons, or explosives without a licence, posing a threat to public safety.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Note all details of the suspected illegal arms holder: identity, location, type of weapon if known.
Step 2 – Call Police Emergency (112) if there is immediate danger.
Step 3 – File a written complaint at the local police station.
Step 4 – If local police are non-responsive, file a complaint with the Superintendent of Police.
Step 5 – Report to the District Magistrate who is the licensing authority for arms.
Step 6 – File a complaint on the state police complaint portal.
Step 7 – Report to the Collector's office if illegal arms are being used to intimidate voters or communities.
required_documents: Written complaint with details of the accused
Any evidence of illegal weapon possession
escalation_path: Local Police → SP → District Magistrate → State Intelligence
complaint_template:
To,
The Station House Officer,
[Police Station]

Subject: Complaint regarding illegal possession of arms/weapons

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], wish to report that [Name/Unknown Person] residing at [Address] is allegedly in illegal possession of [type of weapon]. This is a threat to public safety and violates the Arms Act, 1959.

I request police action to verify and seize the illegal weapons.

I wish to remain anonymous / My contact is [Number].

[Name / Anonymous]
[Date]

subcategory: अवैध शराब / नशीले पदार्थ बिक्री शिकायत
problem_description: A citizen is aware of illegal liquor brewing, sale of illicit alcohol, or narcotics being sold in their area and wants to report it to authorities.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Collect details: location of sale, persons involved, timings.
Step 2 – Call the Police Emergency (112) or local police station.
Step 3 – File a complaint with the Excise Department for illegal liquor.
Step 4 – File a complaint with the Narcotics Control Bureau (NCB) for drug activity.
Step 5 – Report to the District Collector who coordinates anti-liquor and anti-drug operations.
Step 6 – Request confidential treatment of your complaint to protect your identity.
required_documents: Written complaint with location and persons involved
Identity proof (can be kept confidential on request)
escalation_path: Police → Excise Department / NCB → District Collector → State ATS
complaint_template:
To,
The Station House Officer,
[Police Station]

Subject: Confidential complaint regarding illegal liquor/drug activity

Respected Sir/Madam,

I wish to report that illegal liquor/narcotics are being sold at [Location] by [person/unknown] at [timings]. This activity is causing harm to the community, especially youth.

I request police action on this information and ask that my identity be kept confidential.

[Anonymous / Name on request]
[Date]

problem_description: A family member or known person has been kidnapped, abducted, or forcibly taken away by unknown or known persons for ransom or other criminal purposes.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Call Police Emergency (112) immediately – time is critical.
Step 2 – File an FIR at the police station for kidnapping under Section 363 IPC.
Step 3 – Provide all details: last known location, description, vehicle numbers if known.
Step 4 – Do NOT pay ransom before informing police – cooperate with police in tracking.
Step 5 – Request police to involve the Anti-Kidnapping Cell and CID.
Step 6 – Approach the SP/DIG for immediate senior police intervention.
Step 7 – For minor kidnappings, also contact Childline (1098).
required_documents: Recent photograph of the kidnapped person
Contact details of last persons to see the victim
escalation_path: Police Emergency → SP → State CID Anti-Kidnapping Cell → NIA (if organised crime)
complaint_template:
To,
The Station House Officer,
[Police Station]

Subject: FIR complaint regarding kidnapping

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], wish to report that my [relation] [Name], aged [Age], was kidnapped/abducted on [Date] from [Location]. Last seen wearing [clothing description]. A ransom demand of Rs. [Amount] has / has not been received.

I request immediate FIR registration and rescue operation.

Enclosures: Photograph, identity proof, ransom communication if received

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]
[Mobile Number]

problem_description: A woman is being harassed, molested, or eve-teased in a public place, street, school, college, or public transport and wants to file a police complaint.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Seek help from bystanders and note the accused's details immediately.
Step 2 – Call the Women Helpline (1091) or Police Emergency (112).
Step 3 – File an FIR at the local police station under Section 354 IPC (molestation) or Section 509 IPC (outraging modesty).
Step 4 – Request police to record your statement in the presence of a female police officer.
Step 5 – Apply for a restraining order from the Magistrate Court if the harasser is known.
Step 6 – Approach the Women's Commission or DLSA for additional support.
required_documents: CCTV footage request (if available in area)
escalation_path: Women Helpline → Police Station → Magistrate Court → State Women's Commission
complaint_template:
To,
The Station House Officer,
[Police Station]

Subject: FIR complaint regarding molestation/eve-teasing

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], wish to report that on [Date] at [Location], [Name/Unknown Person] subjected me to molestation/eve-teasing by [describe incident briefly]. This caused me mental distress and violated my dignity.

I request FIR registration under Section 354/509 IPC and appropriate action.

Enclosures: Written account, witness details, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]
[Mobile Number]

subcategory: जुआ / अवैध सट्टेबाजी अड्डे की शिकायत
problem_description: A citizen is aware of an illegal gambling den, betting operation, or online betting racket operating in their area.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Gather details: location, operating timings, persons involved.
Step 2 – Call the local police station and report the gambling den.
Step 3 – File a written complaint with the police under the Public Gambling Act.
Step 4 – Report to the Superintendent of Police if local police are non-responsive.
Step 5 – File on CPGRAMS or state portal if no action is taken.
Step 6 – Request police to conduct a raid and inform them your identity will be protected.
escalation_path: Local Police → SP → State Gambling Enforcement Cell
complaint_template:
To,
The Station House Officer,
[Police Station]

Subject: Complaint regarding illegal gambling/betting activity

Respected Sir/Madam,

I wish to report that illegal gambling / betting activities are being conducted at [Address/Location] by [Name/unknown persons]. This activity is conducted during [timings] and is causing social harm in our area.

I request a police raid and appropriate legal action. My identity may be kept confidential.

[Name / Anonymous]
[Date]

category: सरकारी योजना और सार्वजनिक सेवा समस्याएं
subcategory: जन धन / बैंक खाता खोलने से इनकार
problem_description: A citizen from a rural or low-income background is being denied opening of a Jan Dhan or basic savings account by a bank without valid reason.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Visit the bank branch with your Aadhaar card and request to open a PMJDY account.
Step 2 – If denied, ask the bank manager for the reason in writing.
Step 3 – File a written complaint with the Branch Manager.
Step 4 – File a complaint with the Banking Ombudsman.
Step 5 – Report to the Reserve Bank of India through the CMS Portal.
Step 6 – File on CPGRAMS.
Step 7 – Contact your nearest Business Correspondent (Bank Mitra) for assistance.
escalation_path: Bank Branch → Banking Ombudsman → RBI → Consumer Commission
complaint_template:
To,
The Banking Ombudsman,
[Region]

Subject: Complaint regarding refusal to open Jan Dhan / basic savings account

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], visited [Bank Name], [Branch] on [Date] to open a Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana account with my Aadhaar card. The bank refused to open my account without a valid reason.

I request your intervention to direct the bank to open my account immediately.

Enclosures: Aadhaar copy, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

category: सरकारी योजना और सार्वजनिक सेवा समस्याएं
subcategory: प्रधानमंत्री किसान सम्मान निधि (पीएम-किसान) भुगतान न मिलना
problem_description: A registered farmer has not received the PM-KISAN instalment despite being eligible and registered, or their application was wrongly rejected.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Check your beneficiary status on the PM-KISAN portal (pmkisan.gov.in).
Step 2 – Verify if your Aadhaar and bank account are correctly linked.
Step 3 – If rejected, find out the reason from the Patwari/Tehsildar.
Step 4 – Correct any errors in land records or bank details and resubmit.
Step 5 – File a complaint with the Gram Panchayat and Block Agriculture Officer.
Step 6 – File a complaint on the PM-KISAN portal's grievance section.
Step 7 – File on CPGRAMS if the grievance is not addressed.
escalation_path: Patwari → Block Agriculture Officer → District Collector → CPGRAMS → Ministry of Agriculture
complaint_template:
To,
The Block Agriculture Officer,
[Block], [District]

Subject: Complaint regarding non-receipt of PM-KISAN instalment

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], am a registered farmer under PM-KISAN (Registration No. [Number]). I have not received the [instalment number] instalment of Rs. 2000 for the period [Period]. My Aadhaar and bank account are correctly linked.

I request your office to investigate and ensure the payment is credited to my account.

Enclosures: PM-KISAN registration, Aadhaar, bank passbook, land document

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

category: सरकारी योजना और सार्वजनिक सेवा समस्याएं
subcategory: विकलांगता प्रमाण पत्र जारी न होना / गलत विकलांगता प्रतिशत
problem_description: A person with disability has been denied a disability certificate, or the certificate shows a wrong/lower disability percentage, affecting their access to reservations, pensions, and schemes.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Visit the Government Hospital/Medical Board for disability assessment.
Step 2 – If the certificate is denied or percentage is incorrectly assessed, file a written complaint with the Civil Surgeon / Medical Superintendent.
Step 3 – Apply for a review by a higher Medical Board.
Step 4 – File a complaint with the District Social Welfare Officer.
Step 5 – File a complaint with the State Commissioner for Persons with Disabilities.
Step 6 – Approach the Chief Commissioner for Persons with Disabilities (national level) under the RPwD Act.
Step 7 – File a complaint with DLSA for free legal support.

authorities.name: State Commissioner for Persons with Disabilities
Chief Commissioner for Persons with Disabilities

escalation_path: Medical Board → Civil Surgeon → State Disability Commissioner → Chief Commissioner → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The District Social Welfare Officer,
[District]

Subject: Complaint regarding denial / incorrect assessment of disability certificate

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], attended the Medical Board at [Hospital Name] on [Date] for assessment of my disability. My disability certificate was denied / assessed at [X]% which is incorrect as my actual disability is [describe]. Medical reports support a higher percentage.

I request a review by a higher Medical Board and correct issuance of my disability certificate.

Enclosures: Medical reports, application, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

category: सरकारी योजना और सार्वजनिक सेवा समस्याएं
subcategory: आय प्रमाण पत्र जारी न होना / देरी
problem_description: A citizen needs an income certificate for school admissions, government schemes, or reservations but the Tehsildar office is delaying or refusing to issue it.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Submit a formal application with all supporting documents at the Tehsildar or Revenue Office.
Step 2 – Obtain the application acknowledgment with date.
Step 3 – If not issued within the stipulated time (15–30 days typically), file a complaint with the Tehsildar.
Step 4 – Escalate to the Sub-Divisional Magistrate if the Tehsildar is non-responsive.
Step 5 – File a complaint on the state e-District portal or CPGRAMS.
Step 6 – File an RTI application about the status of your application.
Step 7 – Approach DLSA if urgent legal action is needed.
required_documents: Income proof (salary slip, income tax return, employer certificate)
escalation_path: Tehsildar → SDM → District Collector → CPGRAMS → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Sub-Divisional Magistrate,
[Sub-Division], [District]

Subject: Complaint regarding non-issuance of income certificate

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], submitted an application for an income certificate at [Tehsildar Office] on [Date] (Acknowledgment No. [Number]). Despite [Number] days passing, the certificate has not been issued, which is urgently needed for [purpose].

I request your intervention to direct the Tehsildar to issue the certificate immediately.

Enclosures: Application acknowledgment, income proof, Aadhaar

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

category: सरकारी योजना और सार्वजनिक सेवा समस्याएं
problem_description: A government official is demanding a bribe to process a routine application, issue a certificate, or provide a service that is legally free or has a fixed fee.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Do NOT pay the bribe if at all possible.
Step 2 – Contact the Anti-Corruption Bureau (ACB) / Vigilance Department to arrange a trap.
Step 3 – The ACB can conduct a sting operation to catch the official red-handed.
Step 4 – File a complaint with the District Collector / Divisional Commissioner.
Step 5 – File a complaint on the State Vigilance Portal.
Step 6 – Call the Anti-Corruption Helpline (1064).
Step 7 – File a complaint with the Central Vigilance Commission if a central government employee is involved.
required_documents: Written complaint describing the bribe demand
Name and designation of the official
Application or file number being processed
escalation_path: ACB Trap → Prosecution → Sessions Court → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Anti-Corruption Bureau,
[State]

Subject: Complaint regarding bribery demand by government official

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], wish to report that [Official Name / Designation] at [Office Name] is demanding a bribe of Rs. [Amount] to process my application for [Service/Certificate]. This amount is not the legitimate fee.

I request the ACB to take action including a trap operation to catch the official.

Enclosures: Application copy, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]
[Mobile Number – kept confidential if requested]

category: सरकारी योजना और सार्वजनिक सेवा समस्याएं
subcategory: मतदाता पहचान पत्र जारी न होना / मतदाता सूची में गलती
problem_description: A citizen has not received their Voter ID card, or their name has been wrongly deleted from the electoral roll, or the voter roll has incorrect information.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Check your voter registration status on the National Voters' Service Portal (voters.eci.gov.in).
Step 2 – If not registered, apply online through Form 6 for new registration.
Step 3 – If name is wrongly deleted or there is an error, apply for correction through Form 8.
Step 4 – Submit the form to the Electoral Registration Officer (ERO) at the Tehsildar's office.
Step 5 – If application is rejected or ignored, file a complaint with the District Election Officer.
Step 6 – Escalate to the Chief Electoral Officer of the state.
Step 7 – Contact the Election Commission of India for national-level issues.
escalation_path: ERO → District Election Officer → CEO → Election Commission of India
complaint_template:
To,
The District Election Officer,
[District]

Subject: Complaint regarding non-issuance of Voter ID / electoral roll error

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], applied for voter registration / correction on [Date] through Form [6/8] (Application No. [Number]). My name has not been added to / has been wrongly deleted from / has an error in the electoral roll despite my application.

I request correction of the electoral roll and issuance of my Voter ID card.

Enclosures: Application copy, Aadhaar, age proof, address proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

category: सरकारी योजना और सार्वजनिक सेवा समस्याएं
problem_description: Children at an Anganwadi centre are not receiving nutritious meals, or the mid-day meal scheme at a government school is not being implemented properly – food quality is poor, or meals are not being served.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Document the problem: note dates when meals were not served or quality was poor.
Step 2 – File a complaint with the Anganwadi Supervisor or School Headmaster.
Step 3 – Report to the Child Development Project Officer (CDPO) for Anganwadi issues.
Step 4 – Report to the Block Education Officer for school mid-day meal issues.
Step 5 – File a complaint with the District Women and Child Development Officer.
Step 6 – File on CPGRAMS or the state portal.
Step 7 – Contact the National Commission for Protection of Child Rights (NCPCR).
required_documents: Written complaint to Anganwadi Supervisor / School Head

authorities.name: District Women and Child Development Officer

escalation_path: Anganwadi Supervisor/School Head → CDPO/BEO → District WCD Officer → NCPCR → CPGRAMS
complaint_template:
To,
The Child Development Project Officer,
[Block], [District]

Subject: Complaint regarding failure of Anganwadi / mid-day meal service

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], wish to report that the Anganwadi centre / School at [Location] has not been providing nutritious meals / any meals to children for [duration]. The food provided is of very poor quality / is insufficient.

I request your inspection and immediate corrective action to ensure children receive their entitlements.

Enclosures: Written complaint, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

category: सरकारी योजना और सार्वजनिक सेवा समस्याएं
subcategory: प्रधानमंत्री उज्ज्वला योजना एलपीजी कनेक्शन जारी न होना
problem_description: A woman from a BPL household is eligible for a free LPG connection under PM Ujjwala Yojana but has not received it, or the connection is being denied without reason.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Check eligibility on the PMUY portal.
Step 2 – Submit an application at the nearest LPG distributor with required documents.
Step 3 – If application is not processed within 30 days, file a complaint with the oil company helpline.
Step 4 – File a complaint with the District Supply Officer.
Step 5 – File a complaint on CPGRAMS.
Step 6 – Contact the local MP/MLA's office for assistance.
Step 7 – File on the Ministry of Petroleum's portal.
escalation_path: LPG Distributor → Oil Company → District Supply Officer → Ministry of Petroleum → CPGRAMS
complaint_template:
To,
The Customer Care,
[Oil Company Name]

Subject: Complaint regarding non-issuance of PMUY LPG connection

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], applied for an LPG connection under PM Ujjwala Yojana at [Distributor Name] on [Date] (Application No. [Number]). I am a BPL household and eligible for the scheme. Despite [Number] days passing, the connection has not been issued.

I request immediate issuance of my PMUY connection.

Enclosures: BPL proof, Aadhaar, bank passbook, application acknowledgment

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

subcategory: घटिया / रहने योग्य न किराये का आवास
problem_description: A landlord is providing rental accommodation that is in extremely poor condition – leaking roof, no sanitation, structural damage – and is refusing to carry out necessary repairs.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Document all defects with photographs and written descriptions.
Step 2 – Send a written notice to the landlord describing the defects and requesting repairs within 30 days.
Step 3 – If no action, file a complaint with the Rent Controller citing breach of landlord's duty to maintain the premises.
Step 4 – File a complaint with the Municipal Corporation for housing/safety violations.
Step 5 – Apply to the Rent Controller for permission to do repairs and deduct cost from rent.
Step 6 – File a civil suit for rent reduction or termination of lease.
Step 7 – Contact DLSA for free legal aid.
escalation_path: Landlord Notice → Rent Controller → Civil Court → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Rent Controller,
[District]

Subject: Complaint regarding landlord's failure to maintain rented premises

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], am a tenant at [Address] paying rent of Rs. [Amount] per month. The premises are in extremely poor condition with [describe defects: leaking roof / broken plumbing / structural cracks]. Despite a written notice dated [Date], my landlord has refused to carry out repairs.

I request the Rent Controller to direct the landlord to carry out necessary repairs.

Enclosures: Rent agreement, photographs, written notice, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

problem_description: A commercial tenant (shop, office, or business premises) is being forcibly evicted or harassed by the landlord despite having a valid lease agreement.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Collect all commercial lease documents, rent receipts, and payment records.
Step 2 – Send a legal notice to the landlord invoking your rights under the lease and Rent Control Act.
Step 3 – File a complaint with police if the landlord uses force or threatens eviction.
Step 4 – File a petition with the Rent Controller (Commercial) for protection.
Step 5 – Apply for an injunction in Civil Court to restrain illegal eviction.
Step 6 – Negotiate with the landlord for a mutually agreeable exit/settlement.
Step 7 – Approach DLSA or a commercial lawyer for legal representation.
escalation_path: Rent Controller → Civil Court → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Rent Controller,
[District]

Subject: Petition against illegal eviction of commercial tenant

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], operate a [type of business] at [Address] under a valid lease agreement dated [Date] with landlord [Name]. The landlord is attempting to evict me illegally before the lease expires / without following the eviction procedure under the Rent Control Act.

I request the Rent Controller to stay the eviction and protect my tenancy rights.

Enclosures: Lease agreement, rent receipts, business documents, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

subcategory: किरायेदार द्वारा अनाधिकृत उप-किराए पर देने का विवाद
problem_description: A landlord discovers that the tenant has illegally sub-let the property to another person without the landlord's consent, violating the rental agreement.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Verify that sub-letting is not permitted under the rental agreement.
Step 2 – Issue a written legal notice to the tenant to vacate the sub-tenant.
Step 3 – File an eviction petition with the Rent Controller citing unauthorized sub-letting as grounds.
Step 4 – The Rent Controller will conduct inquiry and pass eviction order.
Step 5 – Execute the eviction order through court if tenant does not comply.
Step 6 – Seek damages in Civil Court for breach of lease terms.
required_documents: Evidence of sub-letting (sub-tenant's presence, proof of payments)
escalation_path: Legal Notice → Rent Controller → Civil Court → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Rent Controller,
[District]

Subject: Eviction petition for unauthorized sub-letting

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], am the owner of property at [Address] rented to [Tenant Name] under a rental agreement dated [Date] which expressly prohibits sub-letting. I have discovered that the tenant has illegally sub-let the premises to [Sub-Tenant Name] without my consent.

I request the Rent Controller to pass an eviction order on grounds of unauthorized sub-letting.

Enclosures: Rental agreement, evidence of sub-letting, legal notice, property documents

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

subcategory: मकान मालिक द्वारा खाली करवाने के लिए उपयोगिताएं रोकना
problem_description: A landlord has disconnected electricity, water, or other utilities to the tenant's premises to force the tenant to vacate, without following proper legal eviction procedures.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Document the utility disconnection with photographs and bills.
Step 2 – Send a written notice to the landlord demanding immediate restoration of utilities.
Step 3 – File a police complaint for criminal intimidation and obstruction.
Step 4 – File a complaint with the Rent Controller for breach of tenancy rights.
Step 5 – Apply to the Civil Court for an urgent injunction ordering restoration of utilities.
Step 6 – Contact the utility company (electricity/water) directly to report unauthorized disconnection.
required_documents: Utility bills showing account is in order
escalation_path: Police → Rent Controller → Civil Court (Urgent Injunction)
complaint_template:
To,
The Station House Officer,
[Police Station]

Subject: Complaint regarding landlord disconnecting utilities to force eviction

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], am a tenant at [Address] under a valid rent agreement. My landlord [Name] has disconnected [electricity/water/both] on [Date] to pressure me into vacating the premises without following the legal eviction procedure.

This is criminal intimidation and harassment. I request police intervention and immediate restoration of utilities.

Enclosures: Rent agreement, utility bill, photographs, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

subcategory: मकान किराया भत्ता (एचआरए) किराया रसीद विवाद
problem_description: An employee is unable to claim their HRA tax exemption because the landlord refuses to provide a valid rent receipt or PAN number as required for tax purposes.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Send a written request to the landlord for rent receipts and PAN.
Step 2 – If rent exceeds Rs. 1 lakh per year, inform the employer that landlord PAN is needed.
Step 3 – Collect all payment evidence: bank transfer records, cheque copies.
Step 4 – File a complaint with the Income Tax Department about the landlord's non-compliance.
Step 5 – Consult a tax advisor about alternative ways to claim HRA exemption.
Step 6 – File a civil suit against the landlord for breach of contract if rent receipts are part of the agreement.
required_documents: Bank transfer records showing rent payments
escalation_path: Income Tax Complaint → Civil Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Income Tax Officer,
[City]

Subject: Complaint regarding landlord refusing to provide PAN for HRA exemption

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], am paying rent of Rs. [Amount] per month to landlord [Name] at [Address]. Despite written requests, the landlord has refused to provide their PAN number, preventing me from claiming valid HRA tax exemption.

I request appropriate action against the landlord for non-compliance with income tax requirements.

Enclosures: Rent agreement, bank transfer records, written request to landlord

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

subcategory: किरायेदार द्वारा संपत्ति छोड़ना और बकाया छोड़ना
problem_description: A tenant has vacated the property without notice, left behind unpaid rent, damaged property, or abandoned belongings creating legal complications for the landlord.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Document the abandoned property condition with photographs.
Step 2 – Send a legal notice to the tenant's last known address and email.
Step 3 – Wait for a reasonable period for the tenant to collect their belongings.
Step 4 – File a recovery suit in Civil Court for unpaid rent and damages.
Step 5 – File a complaint with the police if the tenant abandoned under suspicious circumstances.
Step 6 – Apply to the court for permission to dispose of abandoned property after legal notice.
escalation_path: Legal Notice → Civil Court → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Civil Court,
[District]

Subject: Recovery suit for unpaid rent and damages from absconding tenant

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], am the owner of property at [Address]. My tenant [Name] vacated the premises on [Date] without notice, leaving unpaid rent of Rs. [Amount] and causing property damage valued at Rs. [Amount].

Despite legal notice, the tenant has not responded. I request the court to pass a recovery decree for Rs. [Total Amount].

Enclosures: Rental agreement, rent records, photographs, legal notice

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

subcategory: नदी तल / बाढ़ क्षेत्र पर अतिक्रमण
problem_description: Private individuals, industries, or developers have encroached on riverbed land or floodplains, causing flooding risk and environmental damage.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Document the encroachment with photographs and GPS location if possible.
Step 2 – File a complaint with the District Collector who is the riverbed authority.
Step 3 – Report to the Irrigation Department / Water Resources Department.
Step 4 – File a complaint with the State Pollution Control Board.
Step 5 – Approach the National Green Tribunal (NGT) for environmental protection orders.
Step 6 – File a PIL in the High Court for widespread violations.
Step 7 – Report to the National Disaster Management Authority if flooding risk is immediate.
required_documents: Revenue records showing riverbed land classification
escalation_path: District Collector → Water Resources Department → State PCB → NGT → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The District Collector,
[District]

Subject: Complaint regarding encroachment on riverbed / floodplain

Respected Sir/Madam,

I wish to report that [individual/company] has illegally encroached on the riverbed / floodplain of [River Name] near [Location]. This encroachment poses serious flood risk and is causing environmental damage.

I request immediate removal of the encroachment and action against the encroachers.

Enclosures: Photographs, revenue records, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

subcategory: पेड़ काटना / वनों की कटाई शिकायत
problem_description: Trees are being illegally cut down in urban areas, forest areas, or private land without proper permission, violating tree preservation laws.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Photograph the tree cutting with date stamps.
Step 2 – File a complaint with the Municipal Corporation's Tree Authority (for urban trees).
Step 3 – File a complaint with the Forest Department for forest areas.
Step 4 – File a complaint with the District Collector.
Step 5 – Report to the State Pollution Control Board or Environment Department.
Step 6 – File a complaint with the National Green Tribunal.
Step 7 – File a PIL in the High Court for large-scale deforestation.
escalation_path: Municipal Corporation → Forest Department → District Collector → NGT → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Tree Authority,
[Municipal Corporation]

Subject: Complaint regarding illegal tree felling

Respected Sir/Madam,

I wish to report that trees are being illegally cut at [Location] without permission from the Tree Authority / Forest Department. This is in violation of the Maharashtra (Trees) (Preservation and Protection) Act / [applicable state act].

I request immediate inspection and action to stop the illegal felling and prosecute the offenders.

Enclosures: Photographs, identity proof, location details

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

problem_description: A factory or industrial unit is dumping chemical or industrial waste in open fields, water bodies, or roadside areas, causing environmental and health hazards.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Photograph the waste dumping and note the source.
Step 2 – Collect samples if safely possible for lab testing.
Step 3 – Report to the State Pollution Control Board immediately.
Step 4 – File a complaint with the District Collector.
Step 5 – File a complaint with the National Green Tribunal.
Step 6 – Report to the Central Pollution Control Board for severe violations.
Step 7 – File a PIL in High Court for environmental remediation and compensation.
required_documents: Location and details of polluting factory
escalation_path: State PCB → CPCB → District Collector → NGT → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Regional Officer,
State Pollution Control Board

Subject: Complaint regarding illegal industrial waste dumping

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], wish to report that [Factory Name] at [Address] is illegally dumping chemical/industrial waste at [Location] since [Date]. This is causing serious environmental damage and health hazards to residents of [Area Name].

I request immediate inspection and closure of the polluting unit until proper waste disposal systems are installed.

Enclosures: Photographs, sample reports, health impact evidence, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

subcategory: आवारा पशु समस्या / सड़क पर मवेशी शिकायत
problem_description: Stray animals, abandoned cattle, or roaming dogs are causing accidents, injuries, and public nuisance in residential areas or highways.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Report to the local Municipal Corporation Animal Control Department.
Step 2 – File a complaint with the Gram Panchayat for rural areas.
Step 3 – If stray cattle is causing accidents, report to the police and traffic department.
Step 4 – Report to the State Animal Welfare Board.
Step 5 – If bitten by a stray dog, seek medical attention and file a complaint with the Municipal Corporation.
Step 6 – File a complaint with the District Collector if large-scale stray animal problem exists.
Step 7 – File on CPGRAMS if municipal authority is non-responsive.
required_documents: Photographs of stray animals causing nuisance
escalation_path: Municipal Corporation → District Collector → State Animal Welfare Board → High Court PIL
complaint_template:
To,
The Health Officer,
[Municipal Corporation / Gram Panchayat]

Subject: Complaint regarding stray animal menace

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], residing at [Address], wish to report a serious stray animal menace in our area. [Stray dogs/cattle] have been causing [injuries/accidents/property damage] at [Location] since [Date].

I request immediate steps to remove stray animals, provide sterilization/vaccination programmes, and ensure public safety.

Enclosures: Photographs, medical report if applicable, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

problem_description: Unauthorized stone quarrying, rock blasting, or mineral extraction near residential areas is causing noise, vibration, dust pollution, and structural damage to houses.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Document the unauthorized quarrying with photographs, note blast timings and impact.
Step 2 – Report structural damage to homes with photographs and engineer assessment.
Step 3 – File a complaint with the District Mines and Geology Department.
Step 4 – File a complaint with the District Collector.
Step 5 – File a complaint with the State Pollution Control Board for dust and noise.
Step 6 – File a complaint with the National Green Tribunal.
Step 7 – File a PIL in the High Court for immediate stoppage and compensation.
required_documents: Photographs of quarrying activity and damage
escalation_path: District Mines Dept → District Collector → State PCB → NGT → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The District Collector / District Mines Officer,
[District]

Subject: Complaint regarding unauthorized stone quarrying / mining

Respected Sir/Madam,

Residents of [Village/Area Name] are suffering due to unauthorized stone quarrying at [Location] being conducted by [operator name if known]. The blasting is causing structural damage to our homes and serious health problems due to dust.

I request immediate stoppage of the unauthorized quarrying and compensation for damages.

Enclosures: Photographs, damage assessment, witness statements, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

subcategory: खुली जलन / पराली जलाने की शिकायत
problem_description: Agricultural stubble burning or open waste burning is causing serious air pollution and health problems for nearby communities.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Photograph the burning and note the location and timing.
Step 2 – Report to the State Pollution Control Board.
Step 3 – Report to the local police station.
Step 4 – Report to the Block Development Officer or District Agriculture Officer.
Step 5 – File a complaint with the District Collector.
Step 6 – File a complaint on CPGRAMS.
Step 7 – Report to CPCB or NGT if the problem is widespread.
required_documents: Medical records if health impact is documented
escalation_path: State PCB → District Collector → CPCB → NGT → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The District Agriculture Officer / District Collector,
[District]

Subject: Complaint regarding stubble / open waste burning

Respected Sir/Madam,

I wish to report that stubble burning / open burning of waste is occurring at [Location] since [Date]. This is causing severe air pollution and health problems including respiratory issues for residents of [Area Name].

I request immediate action to stop the burning and awareness campaigns about alternative stubble management.

Enclosures: Photographs, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

subcategory: स्ट्रीट लाइट / सार्वजनिक बुनियादी ढांचा विफलता शिकायत
problem_description: Street lights are not functioning, public roads have deep potholes, or other public infrastructure failures are causing accidents and public safety hazards.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Document the problem: photograph broken street lights, potholes, with location details.
Step 2 – File a complaint with the Municipal Corporation / Gram Panchayat.
Step 3 – File with the Public Works Department (PWD) for road-related issues.
Step 4 – File a complaint on the city's civic app or portal (e.g., MyGov, Swachhata App).
Step 5 – File on CPGRAMS if the municipal authority is non-responsive.
Step 6 – If accidents have occurred due to the infrastructure failure, file a police complaint for negligence.
required_documents: Photographs of infrastructure failure with location
escalation_path: Municipal Corporation → District Collector → PWD → CPGRAMS → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Executive Engineer,
Public Works Department / Municipal Corporation,
[District]

Subject: Complaint regarding broken street lights / road potholes

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], wish to report that street lights at [Location] have been non-functional / the road at [Location] has deep potholes since [Date]. This is causing accidents and danger to pedestrians and motorists.

I request immediate repair of the infrastructure.

Enclosures: Photographs, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

subcategory: सेवानिवृत्ति के बाद वरिष्ठ नागरिक को पेंशन से वंचित
problem_description: A retired government employee is not receiving their pension, provident fund, or retirement dues from the government despite being eligible.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Collect all retirement documents: service record, PPO (Pension Payment Order).
Step 2 – Verify if pension has been sanctioned on the Pensioners Portal (pensionersportal.gov.in).
Step 3 – File a written complaint with the Accounts Officer / Treasury Officer.
Step 4 – File a complaint with the Department of Pension and Pensioners' Welfare.
Step 5 – File on CPGRAMS.
Step 6 – Contact the CAT (Central Administrative Tribunal) for central government pensioners.
Step 7 – Approach DLSA for free legal aid.

authorities.name: Department of Pension and Pensioners' Welfare
High Court / Administrative Tribunal (for state employees)

escalation_path: Accounts Office → Pension Department → CAT/Administrative Tribunal → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Accounts Officer / Treasury Officer,
[District]

Subject: Complaint regarding non-payment of pension/retirement dues

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], retired from [Department/Post] on [Date] after [Years] of service. Despite my retirement, I have not received my pension / GPF / gratuity dues. My PPO number is [Number].

I request immediate release of my retirement dues.

Enclosures: Service record, PPO, retirement order, bank passbook, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]
[Mobile Number]

subcategory: फंसा बुजुर्ग व्यक्ति / घर वापस न आ पाना
problem_description: An elderly person has been stranded away from home, abandoned by family members, or is unable to find their way home due to memory issues or age-related conditions.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Call the Senior Citizen Helpline (14567) or Childline (1098) if a vulnerable adult is stranded.
Step 2 – The police or helpline will arrange for the person's immediate care and safety.
Step 3 – File a missing person complaint at the local police station if the person has gone missing.
Step 4 – The police will attempt to locate family members through available documents.
Step 5 – If family cannot be found, the District Social Welfare Officer will arrange shelter.
Step 6 – Contact old age homes or government shelter homes for temporary accommodation.
Step 7 – If family abandoned the elderly person intentionally, file a complaint under the Senior Citizens Act.
required_documents: Identity proof of senior citizen (if available)
Any documents found on the person
escalation_path: Senior Citizen Helpline → Police → District Social Welfare → Old Age Home
complaint_template:
To,
The Station House Officer,
[Police Station]

Subject: Complaint regarding abandoned/stranded senior citizen

Respected Sir/Madam,

I am reporting that an elderly person named [Name / Unknown], approximately [Age] years old, has been found stranded/abandoned at [Location]. The person appears to be in need of immediate care and assistance.

I request police assistance to identify the person, contact their family, and arrange appropriate care.

Yours faithfully,
[Name of Informant]
[Date]
[Mobile Number]

subcategory: वरिष्ठ नागरिक को अवैध हिरासत / गलत बंदी में रखना
problem_description: An elderly person is being kept in illegal confinement by family members, caretakers, or others against their will, often to control their assets.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Call Police Emergency (112) if immediate danger is suspected.
Step 2 – File a Habeas Corpus petition in the High Court for release from unlawful confinement.
Step 3 – File a complaint with the Senior Citizen Protection Cell.
Step 4 – File an FIR at the police station under Section 340 IPC (wrongful confinement).
Step 5 – Approach the Sub-Divisional Magistrate for an emergency welfare order.
Step 6 – Contact the District Legal Services Authority for free legal assistance.
Step 7 – Approach the National Human Rights Commission if fundamental rights are violated.
required_documents: Evidence of confinement (witness statements, inability to contact)
escalation_path: Police → SDM → High Court (Habeas Corpus) → NHRC
complaint_template:
To,
The Station House Officer,
[Police Station]

Subject: Complaint regarding wrongful confinement of senior citizen

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], wish to report that [Senior Citizen Name], aged [Age], is being kept in unlawful confinement at [Address] by [Confiner's Name] against their will. The elderly person has not been allowed to communicate or leave freely.

I request immediate police investigation and release of the senior citizen.

Enclosures: Identity proof, relationship proof, evidence of confinement

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]
[Mobile Number]

problem_description: An elderly person in a paid nursing home or old age home is being neglected, abused, or denied promised services, and the facility is not maintaining minimum care standards.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Document all concerns about the facility: poor food, lack of medical care, physical conditions.
Step 2 – Raise concerns with the Facility Manager or Administration in writing.
Step 3 – File a complaint with the Senior Citizen Helpline (14567).
Step 4 – File a complaint with the District Social Welfare Officer.
Step 5 – Report to the State Health Department if medical neglect is involved.
Step 6 – File a consumer complaint in the Consumer Commission for deficient service.
Step 7 – File a police complaint if abuse is occurring.
Step 8 – Move the senior citizen to a safe alternative facility if necessary.
required_documents: Admission agreement with the nursing home
escalation_path: Facility Management → District Social Welfare → State Health Dept → Consumer Commission → Police
complaint_template:
To,
The District Social Welfare Officer,
[District]

Subject: Complaint regarding neglect/abuse of senior citizen in nursing home

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], am the [relation] of [Senior Citizen Name] who is a resident at [Nursing Home Name], [Address]. I have observed that my [relation] is being neglected / abused / denied promised medical care at the facility.

I request your authority to inspect the facility and ensure the wellbeing of all residents.

Enclosures: Admission agreement, medical records, evidence of neglect, payment receipts

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]
[Mobile Number]

subcategory: धोखाधड़ीपूर्ण धार्मिक / धर्मार्थ संस्था द्वारा वरिष्ठ नागरिक को निशाना
problem_description: An elderly person is being exploited by a fake religious organisation or fraudulent charity that is manipulating them into donating large sums of money or property.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Gather evidence of the fraudulent organisation and their communications with the senior citizen.
Step 2 – Speak with the senior citizen calmly and provide them with factual information.
Step 3 – Call the Senior Citizen Helpline (14567) for counselling and guidance.
Step 4 – File a police FIR for cheating and fraud under Section 420 IPC.
Step 5 – File a complaint with the Charity Commissioner against the fraudulent organisation.
Step 6 – Approach the Civil Court to challenge any property transfers made under undue influence.
Step 7 – File a complaint with the Senior Citizen Protection Cell.
escalation_path: Senior Citizen Helpline → Police → Charity Commissioner → Civil Court → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Station House Officer,
[Police Station]

Subject: Complaint regarding fraud by fake religious/charitable organisation targeting senior citizen

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], wish to report that [Organisation Name] has been fraudulently exploiting my [relation] [Senior Citizen Name], aged [Age], by inducing donations of Rs. [Amount] through false religious/charitable claims.

I request FIR registration and investigation of the fraudulent organisation.

Enclosures: Evidence of fraud, donation receipts, communications, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

subcategory: कार्यस्थल पर आयु भेदभाव का सामना करने वाले वरिष्ठ नागरिक
problem_description: An elderly worker or senior professional is facing discrimination, forced retirement, or denial of employment opportunities based solely on their age.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Document all instances of age discrimination with dates and witnesses.
Step 2 – Review employment contract for retirement age and terms.
Step 3 – File a complaint with the Labour Commissioner for age-based employment discrimination.
Step 4 – Raise an industrial dispute if unlawfully forced to retire.
Step 5 – File a complaint with the National Commission for Senior Citizens (if applicable in state).
Step 6 – Approach the Labour Court or Administrative Tribunal.
Step 7 – File a writ petition in the High Court if fundamental rights are violated.
escalation_path: Labour Commissioner → Labour Court → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Labour Commissioner,
[District]

Subject: Complaint regarding age-based employment discrimination

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], aged [Age] years, am employed at [Company Name] as [Designation]. I am being discriminated against based solely on my age by [describe: forced to retire early / denied promotion / excluded from opportunities / threatened with termination].

I request your office to investigate this age discrimination and direct my employer to stop this practice.

Enclosures: Employment contract, evidence of discrimination, service records, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

subcategory: टेलीमार्केटिंग घोटाले का शिकार वरिष्ठ नागरिक
problem_description: An elderly person has been repeatedly called by telemarketers or scammers selling fake health products, insurance, or investment schemes, and has lost money to these scams.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Register on the National Do Not Call Registry (NDNC) by calling 1909.
Step 2 – Document all fraudulent telemarketing calls and transactions.
Step 3 – Call the Cyber Fraud Helpline (1930) if money was lost.
Step 4 – File a complaint on the National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal.
Step 5 – File a complaint with TRAI against the telecom operator for allowing spam calls.
Step 6 – File a consumer complaint for fraudulent goods/services purchased.
Step 7 – File a police FIR if the fraud amount is significant.
escalation_path: NDNC Registration → Cyber Fraud Helpline → TRAI → Consumer Commission → Police FIR
complaint_template:
To,
The Station House Officer,
Cyber Crime Police Station

Subject: Complaint regarding telemarketing fraud targeting senior citizen

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name / Name of senior citizen], aged [Age], have been repeatedly contacted by fraudulent telemarketers who sold me fake [health products / insurance / investment scheme] for Rs. [Amount]. The products/services were never delivered / were worthless.

I request investigation and recovery of my money.

Enclosures: Call records, payment receipts, bank statements, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]

problem_description: A couple or individual faces threats of violence, harassment, or death from family members who oppose their relationship or marriage on grounds of caste, religion, or community.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Call Police Emergency (112) immediately if there is a threat to life.
Step 2 – Approach the nearest police station and file an FIR.
Step 3 – Request police protection and safe accommodation.
Step 4 – File a petition in the High Court for protection order under the Special Marriage Act if applicable.
Step 5 – Approach the District Magistrate for police protection.
Step 6 – Contact the State Women's Commission for immediate intervention.
Step 7 – Seek free legal aid from DLSA.
Step 8 – Contact NGOs working on inter-caste or inter-religious marriages for safe house assistance.
required_documents: Evidence of threats (messages, calls, witness accounts)
escalation_path: Police Emergency → SP → District Magistrate → High Court Protection Order → NHRC
complaint_template:
To,
The Superintendent of Police,
[District]

Subject: Complaint regarding threat to life from family members (honour crime)

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], am facing serious threats to my life from [family members' names] because of my relationship/marriage with [Partner's Name]. They have threatened to harm/kill us on grounds of [caste/religion/community].

I request immediate police protection, registration of FIR, and safe accommodation.

Enclosures: Evidence of threats, identity proof, marriage documents

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]
[Mobile Number]

problem_description: A married woman's jewellery, gifts, or Streedhan (personal property given at marriage) has been taken or retained by her husband or in-laws and is being refused to be returned.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Prepare a list of all Streedhan items with their approximate values and any available proof (photographs, receipts).
Step 2 – Send a legal notice to the husband/in-laws demanding return of Streedhan.
Step 3 – File a complaint with the Protection Officer under the Domestic Violence Act for recovery of Streedhan.
Step 4 – File a police complaint under Section 406 IPC (criminal breach of trust) for retention of Streedhan.
Step 5 – Apply to the Magistrate Court for a monetary relief order under the DV Act.
Step 6 – File a civil suit for recovery of Streedhan.
Step 7 – Contact DLSA for free legal representation.
escalation_path: Police FIR → Protection Officer → Magistrate Court → Family Court → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Station House Officer,
[Police Station]

Subject: Complaint regarding retention of Streedhan by husband / in-laws

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], am the wife of [Husband's Name]. My Streedhan including jewellery and gifts valued at approximately Rs. [Amount], given to me at the time of my marriage, has been retained by my husband / in-laws and is being refused to be returned.

I request FIR registration under Section 406 IPC (criminal breach of trust) and action to recover my Streedhan.

Enclosures: Streedhan list, photographs, marriage certificate, identity proof

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]
[Mobile Number]

subcategory: माता-पिता द्वारा अपहरण / बिना सहमति के बच्चे को विदेश ले जाना
problem_description: One parent has taken a child to another state or country without the other parent's consent or in violation of a custody court order.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – File a police complaint immediately for child kidnapping.
Step 2 – File a Habeas Corpus petition in the High Court for return of the child.
Step 3 – Inform the passport authority to flag the child's passport for international cases.
Step 4 – Contact the Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) for children taken abroad.
Step 5 – Apply to the Family Court for enforcement of existing custody order.
Step 6 – Contact DLSA for urgent free legal representation.
Step 7 – File with Interpol through Indian police for international cases.
required_documents: Evidence that child was taken without consent
escalation_path: Police → Family Court → High Court (Habeas Corpus) → MEA → Interpol
complaint_template:
To,
The Station House Officer,
[Police Station]

Subject: FIR complaint regarding parental kidnapping of child

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], am the [mother/father] of [Child's Name] born on [Date]. My [spouse/ex-spouse] [Name] has taken our child to [Location/Country] on [Date] without my consent and in violation of [existing court order / my parental rights].

I request immediate FIR registration and action to ensure return of my child.

Enclosures: Child's birth certificate, custody order, identity proof, evidence

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]
[Mobile Number]

subcategory: पति/पत्नी द्वारा मानसिक क्रूरता और मनोवैज्ञानिक दुर्व्यवहार
problem_description: A spouse is subjected to continuous mental cruelty, emotional abuse, humiliation, isolation, or psychological manipulation by the other spouse or in-laws.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Keep a detailed written diary of all incidents with dates and descriptions.
Step 2 – Save all evidence of mental cruelty: abusive messages, emails, witness accounts.
Step 3 – Seek counselling from a psychologist and obtain a psychological impact report.
Step 4 – File a complaint with the Protection Officer under the DV Act (mental abuse is covered).
Step 5 – File for a Protection Order from the Magistrate Court.
Step 6 – File for divorce on grounds of mental cruelty in Family Court if desired.
Step 7 – Seek free legal aid from DLSA.
Step 8 – Contact the One Stop Centre (181) for comprehensive support.
escalation_path: Protection Officer → Magistrate Court → Family Court → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Protection Officer (DV Act),
[District]

Subject: Complaint regarding mental cruelty and psychological abuse by spouse

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, [Your Name], have been subjected to continuous mental cruelty and psychological abuse by my husband [Name] and/or in-laws since [Date]. The abuse includes [describe: constant humiliation / isolation from family / threats / emotional manipulation].

I request a Protection Order and counselling support under the Domestic Violence Act, 2005.

Enclosures: Written incident diary, evidence of abuse, psychological report, marriage certificate

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Date]
[Mobile Number]

problem_description: After the death of a business owner, legal heirs are fighting over ownership of a shop or commercial property with no registered will.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Obtain death certificate of the deceased owner.
Step 2 – Collect all property ownership documents and revenue records.
Step 3 – Apply for a succession certificate or letters of administration from the Civil Court.
Step 4 – Attempt mediation through DLSA to divide the property amicably.
Step 5 – File a partition suit in Civil Court if mediation fails.
Step 6 – Apply for mutation of the property in the names of legal heirs after court order.
Step 7 – Register the partition deed with the Sub-Registrar.
escalation_path: DLSA Mediation → Civil Court → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Civil Court,
[District]

Subject: Partition suit for commercial property

I, [Your Name], am a legal heir of late [Name] who owned Shop/Property at [Address]. The property has not been divided among legal heirs. I request the court to pass a partition decree.

Enclosures: Death certificate, ownership documents, legal heir certificate

[Name]
[Date]

subcategory: संपत्ति बेचने के लिए फर्जी मुख्तारनामा
problem_description: A fraudster has sold a citizen's property using a forged or fabricated Power of Attorney, transferring ownership without the actual owner's knowledge.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Check property records at the Sub-Registrar office to detect unauthorized transfer.
Step 2 – Obtain a certified copy of the fraudulent Power of Attorney and sale deed.
Step 3 – File an FIR for forgery and fraud under IPC Sections 420, 467, 468.
Step 4 – File a civil suit for cancellation of the fraudulent sale deed.
Step 5 – Apply for an injunction to prevent further transfer of the property.
Step 6 – File a complaint with the District Collector.
Step 7 – Approach DLSA for free legal aid.
required_documents: Copy of fraudulent POA and sale deed
escalation_path: FIR → Civil Court → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Station House Officer,
[Police Station]

Subject: FIR for fraudulent sale using forged Power of Attorney

I, [Your Name], discovered that my property at [Address] has been fraudulently sold using a forged Power of Attorney by [Fraudster Name]. I request FIR registration and immediate action.

Enclosures: Ownership documents, copy of fraudulent deed, identity proof

[Name]
[Date]

subcategory: प्रकाश और वेंटिलेशन अवरुद्ध करने वाला अवैध निर्माण
problem_description: A neighbour has constructed a wall or structure that blocks light, air, or ventilation to the complainant's property, affecting the habitability of their home.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Photograph the illegal construction and its impact on your property.
Step 2 – File a complaint with the Municipal Corporation or Gram Panchayat.
Step 3 – Request the building inspector to verify if the construction violates building bylaws.
Step 4 – File a complaint with the Town Planning Department.
Step 5 – Send a legal notice to the neighbour under the Easements Act (right to light and air).
Step 6 – File a civil suit for injunction and demolition of the illegal structure.
Step 7 – Approach DLSA for free legal representation.
escalation_path: Municipal Corporation → Civil Court → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Building Inspector,
[Municipal Corporation]

Subject: Complaint regarding illegal construction blocking light and ventilation

I, [Your Name], residing at [Address], report that my neighbour [Name] has illegally constructed [describe structure] that blocks light and air to my property in violation of building bylaws and easement rights.

I request inspection and demolition of the illegal structure.

Enclosures: Photographs, property documents, identity proof

[Name]
[Date]

subcategory: दो गांवों / ग्राम पंचायतों के बीच भूमि विवाद
problem_description: Two villages or Gram Panchayats are disputing the boundary of land between them, creating tension and legal confusion over which panchayat has jurisdiction.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Obtain official revenue maps and survey records from the Tehsildar.
Step 2 – Request the Tehsildar to conduct a joint survey of the disputed boundary.
Step 3 – File a complaint with the Sub-Divisional Magistrate for boundary demarcation.
Step 4 – The SDM will appoint a surveyor and conduct hearings from both villages.
Step 5 – Appeal to the District Collector if the SDM's order is disputed.
Step 6 – Escalate to the Revenue Tribunal or High Court if necessary.
escalation_path: Tehsildar → SDM → District Collector → Revenue Tribunal → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Sub-Divisional Magistrate,
[Sub-Division]

Subject: Request for boundary demarcation between villages

We, the representatives of [Village Name], request demarcation of the disputed boundary between our village and [Other Village Name]. Revenue records support our claim to the disputed land.

We request a joint survey and formal demarcation order.

Enclosures: Revenue maps, survey records, historical evidence

[Gram Panchayat President]
[Date]

problem_description: A person or entity holds land in excess of the Urban Land Ceiling Act limits and has not surrendered the surplus land to the government.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Verify the applicable land ceiling limits in your state.
Step 2 – Identify the person/entity holding excess land with revenue records.
Step 3 – File a complaint with the District Collector or Urban Land Ceiling Authority.
Step 4 – The authority will investigate and issue notice to the land holder.
Step 5 – File a PIL in the High Court if authorities are non-responsive.
Step 6 – Report to the state revenue department.

Step 4 – Approach the Minority Commission if the burial ground belongs to a minority community.
Step 5 – File a PIL in the High Court for protection of the burial ground.
Step 6 – Seek support from religious community leaders and local NGOs.

subcategory: नियोक्ता द्वारा वेतन पर टीडीएस / आयकर जमा न करना
problem_description: An employer is deducting TDS from employee salaries but not depositing it with the Income Tax Department, causing employees to face tax demands.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Check your Form 26AS on the Income Tax portal to verify if TDS has been deposited.
Step 2 – Request Form 16 from your employer.
Step 3 – If TDS is not deposited, file a complaint with the Income Tax Department.
Step 4 – File a complaint with the Labour Commissioner for wage-related fraud.
Step 5 – Report to the Tax Deduction Account Number (TAN) section of Income Tax.
Step 6 – Approach the Assessing Officer for relief from tax demand due to employer default.
escalation_path: Income Tax Complaint → Labour Commissioner → ITAT → High Court

I request action against my employer and relief from consequential tax demands on me.

Enclosures: Salary slips, Form 26AS, identity proof

problem_description: A male government or private sector employee is being denied paternity leave that he is legally entitled to after the birth of his child.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Check your entitlement: central government employees get 15 days paternity leave.
Step 2 – Apply formally for paternity leave with the child's birth certificate.
Step 3 – If denied, file a written complaint with HR and request written reasons.
Step 4 – File a grievance with the Labour Commissioner.
Step 5 – File a complaint with the Central/State Government HR Department if a government employee.
Step 6 – Approach the Labour Court if administrative remedy fails.
escalation_path: Labour Commissioner → Labour Court → High Court

I request your office to direct my employer to grant my entitled paternity leave.

Enclosures: Birth certificate, leave application, denial letter, identity proof

[Date]

subcategory: उचित नोटिस या मुआवजे के बिना छंटनी
problem_description: A worker in a company with 100+ workers has been retrenched without the mandatory 3-month notice, government permission, or retrenchment compensation.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Verify the retrenchment letter and check if statutory requirements were followed.
Step 2 – Calculate retrenchment compensation owed (15 days per year of service).
Step 3 – Send a legal notice to the employer demanding compliance.
Step 4 – File a complaint with the Labour Commissioner and raise an industrial dispute.
Step 5 – The Labour Commissioner will attempt conciliation.
Step 6 – File a reference to the Labour Court/Industrial Tribunal if conciliation fails.
Step 7 – Seek free legal aid from DLSA.
escalation_path: Labour Commissioner → Industrial Tribunal → High Court

The Labour Commissioner,

I request initiation of conciliation proceedings and direction to the employer to comply with the Industrial Disputes Act.

Enclosures: Retrenchment letter, service records, salary slips

[Date]

problem_description: A concerned citizen or parent has found children below 14 years working in hazardous occupations or factories, in violation of the Child Labour Act.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Note the details: location, age of children, nature of work.
Step 2 – Call the Childline (1098) for immediate rescue.
Step 3 – File a complaint with the District Magistrate's office.
Step 4 – Report to the Labour Department for action under the Child Labour Act.
Step 5 – Police will raid the workplace and rescue children.
Step 6 – Rescued children are placed under the National Child Labour Project for rehabilitation.
Step 7 – File a complaint with the NCPCR for follow-up.
escalation_path: Childline → Police → Labour Department → NCPCR

The District Magistrate,

I request immediate raid and rescue of the children and prosecution of the employer.

Enclosures: Identity proof, photographs if available

problem_description: Home-based workers or piece-rate workers (beedi workers, incense stick rollers, garment workers) are being paid below the minimum wage for their piece-rate work.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Calculate the piece-rate wages received and compare with the notified minimum wage.
Step 2 – Collect work orders, payment slips, and delivery receipts.
Step 3 – File a complaint with the Labour Commissioner for non-payment of minimum wages.
Step 4 – Contact the Beedi and Cigar Workers Welfare Fund if applicable.
Step 5 – Approach DLSA for free legal representation.
Step 6 – File a complaint in Labour Court for recovery of underpaid wages.
escalation_path: Labour Commissioner → Labour Court → High Court

subcategory: डीपफेक / एआई-जनित सामग्री का ब्लैकमेल में उपयोग
problem_description: A citizen is being blackmailed using AI-generated deepfake videos or images that falsely show them in compromising situations.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Do not pay the blackmailer – paying encourages further demands.
Step 2 – Save all evidence: messages, demands, and the fake content details.
Step 3 – File a complaint on the National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal immediately.
Step 4 – Call the Cyber Fraud Helpline (1930).
Step 5 – File an FIR at the Cyber Crime Police Station under IT Act and IPC.
Step 6 – Report the content to the platform hosting it for removal.
Step 7 – Contact the National Commission for Women if the victim is a woman.
escalation_path: Cyber Crime Portal → Cyber Crime Police → NCW → High Court
complaint_template:
To,
The Station House Officer,
Cyber Crime Police Station

Subject: Complaint regarding deepfake blackmail

I, [Your Name], am being blackmailed by [Name/Unknown] using AI-generated fake videos/images of me. The blackmailer is demanding Rs. [Amount] / [other demands].

I request FIR registration and immediate action to identify and arrest the blackmailer.

Enclosures: Screenshots of demands, identity proof

[Date]
[Mobile Number]

problem_description: A citizen was deceived into scanning a malicious QR code that was sent under false pretenses (refund, KYC, survey), resulting in unauthorized debit from their account.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Call your bank to block the account and report unauthorized debit immediately.
Step 2 – Call Cyber Fraud Helpline (1930) within the first hour for best chance of account freeze.
Step 3 – Save all communication from the fraudster.
Step 4 – File a complaint on the National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal.
Step 5 – File an FIR at the Cyber Crime Police Station.
Step 6 – File a Banking Ombudsman complaint for chargeback of unauthorized transaction.
required_documents: Screenshot of QR code and communication
escalation_path: Cyber Fraud Helpline → Cyber Crime Police → Banking Ombudsman → RBI
complaint_template:
To,
The Station House Officer,
Cyber Crime Police Station

Subject: Complaint regarding QR code fraud

I, [Your Name], was sent a QR code by [fraudster] on [Date] under false pretense of [refund/KYC/survey]. After scanning, Rs. [Amount] was debited from my account without my authorization.

I request FIR registration and recovery of the defrauded amount.

Enclosures: Bank statement, QR code screenshot, communication records, identity proof

[Name]
[Date]

problem_description: A citizen's personal data (bank credentials, Aadhaar, credit card info) has been found being sold on the dark web, indicating a major data breach.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Change all passwords and enable two-factor authentication immediately.
Step 2 – Lock Aadhaar biometrics on the UIDAI portal.
Step 3 – Inform your bank to block cards and issue new ones.
Step 4 – File a complaint with CERT-In.
Step 5 – File a complaint at the Cyber Crime Police Station.
Step 6 – Report to the Data Protection Board once fully operational.
Step 7 – File a complaint against the data-holding company that was breached.
escalation_path: CERT-In → Cyber Crime Police → Data Protection Board
complaint_template:

The Director General, CERT-In

I, [Your Name], discovered that my personal data including [describe: bank credentials / Aadhaar / PAN] is being sold on the dark web. This indicates a data breach at [Company/Portal if known].

I request investigation and action under the IT Act.

Enclosures: Evidence of dark web listing, identity proof

[Date]

problem_description: A fraudster has created a fake WhatsApp or Telegram account using someone's photo and name to ask their contacts for emergency money transfers.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Alert your contacts immediately that your account has been impersonated.
Step 2 – Report the fake account to WhatsApp/Telegram for takedown.
Step 3 – File a complaint on the National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal.
Step 4 – Call Cyber Fraud Helpline (1930) if money has been transferred.
Step 5 – File an FIR at the Cyber Crime Police Station.
Step 6 – If your account was hacked, take steps to recover it immediately.
escalation_path: Cyber Crime Portal → Cyber Crime Police

problem_description: A citizen paid advance rent or deposit for a property found on a rental listing website, only to find the property doesn't exist or the lister was fraudulent.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Stop further payments immediately.
Step 2 – Save all communication, listing details, and payment records.
Step 3 – Call Cyber Fraud Helpline (1930) to attempt account freeze.
Step 4 – File a complaint on the National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal.
Step 5 – File an FIR at the Cyber Crime Police Station.
Step 6 – Report the fraudulent listing to the rental platform.
Step 7 – File a consumer complaint if the platform was negligent in verifying listings.
escalation_path: Cyber Fraud Helpline → Cyber Crime Police → Consumer Commission

subcategory: जिम / फिटनेस सेंटर सदस्यता धनवापसी विवाद
problem_description: A consumer paid for a long-term gym or fitness centre membership but the gym closed down, or is denying services promised, and is refusing to refund the advance fees.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Collect your membership contract, payment receipts, and gym closure/service denial evidence.
Step 2 – Send a written refund request to the gym management.
Step 3 – File a complaint on the National Consumer Helpline (1915).
Step 4 – File a consumer case in the District Consumer Commission.
Step 5 – File a complaint with the local Municipal Corporation if the gym is operating illegally after closure.
Step 6 – If it is a chain gym, escalate to the corporate office.

problem_description: A patient or their family has been overcharged by a private hospital, billed for services not rendered, or charged above the rate capped under government schemes like Ayushman Bharat.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Request an itemized hospital bill and review each charge.
Step 2 – Cross-check against government rate cards (CGHS, Ayushman Bharat rates).
Step 3 – File a written complaint with the Hospital Superintendent.
Step 4 – File a complaint with the State Medical Council.
Step 5 – File a consumer complaint in the District Consumer Commission.
Step 6 – File a complaint with the National Health Authority if an Ayushman Bharat empanelled hospital is involved.
Step 7 – Report to the State Health Department.
escalation_path: Hospital Superintendent → State Medical Council → Consumer Commission → NHA

I request the commission to direct the hospital to refund Rs. [Amount] and pay compensation.

problem_description: A real estate developer delivered flats but has not provided promised amenities like parking, clubhouse, swimming pool, garden, or electricity backup as stated in the sale agreement.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Review the sale agreement for all promised amenities and timelines.
Step 2 – Document which amenities are missing.
Step 3 – Send a legal notice to the builder demanding amenities or compensation.
Step 4 – File a complaint with RERA.
Step 5 – File a consumer complaint in the Consumer Commission.
Step 6 – Approach the Housing Society as a collective action with all affected residents.
Step 7 – File a PIL if the entire housing project is affected.

problem_description: An informal moneylender is charging exorbitant interest rates well above legal limits and using coercive methods to recover loans from vulnerable borrowers.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Document all loan agreements, interest demanded, and repayment records.
Step 2 – File a complaint with the local police against the moneylender for coercive recovery.
Step 3 – Report to the District Collector under the state's Money Lending Act.
Step 4 – Seek intervention from the Debt Recovery Tribunal if large amounts are involved.
Step 5 – Approach DLSA for legal aid.
Step 6 – Contact an NGO or microfinance institution for refinancing at legal rates.
escalation_path: Police → District Collector → DLSA → High Court

problem_description: A passenger cancelled an airline or train ticket within the permitted cancellation period but has not received the refund from the airline or IRCTC.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Collect the cancellation confirmation and track refund status.
Step 2 – File a complaint with the airline's customer service or IRCTC helpdesk.
Step 3 – File a complaint on the Railway's CPGRAMS or airline's regulator (DGCA for airlines).
Step 4 – File a consumer complaint in the District Consumer Commission.
Step 5 – For trains, file a complaint with the Railway Claims Tribunal.
Step 6 – For airlines, file a complaint with DGCA.
escalation_path: Airline/IRCTC → DGCA/Railway → Consumer Commission

problem_description: A citizen was physically assaulted, threatened with weapons, or harassed by another driver or passenger during a road rage incident.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Call Police Emergency (112) immediately after the incident.
Step 2 – Note the vehicle number, description of assailants, and location.
Step 3 – Seek medical attention and obtain a medical report if injured.
Step 4 – File an FIR at the local police station for assault and criminal intimidation.
Step 5 – Request collection of CCTV footage from the area.
Step 6 – File a complaint with the Traffic Police for reckless driving.
Step 7 – Approach the Magistrate for a restraining order if the assailant is known.
escalation_path: Police Station → SP → Magistrate Court

problem_description: A person from a Scheduled Caste or Scheduled Tribe has been subjected to discrimination, humiliation, violence, or social boycott based on their caste identity.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – File an FIR at the police station – the police must register it under the SC/ST (Prevention of Atrocities) Act.
Step 2 – If police refuse, file a written complaint with the SP.
Step 3 – Report to the District SC/ST Welfare Officer.
Step 4 – Apply for interim relief under the SC/ST Act from the District Magistrate.
Step 5 – File a complaint with the National Commission for Scheduled Castes (NCSC).
Step 6 – Approach the Special Court under the SC/ST Act.
Step 7 – Seek free legal aid from DLSA.
escalation_path: Police Station → NCSC → Special Court → High Court
complaint_template:

The Station House Officer,
[Police Station]
