I request FIR registration under the SC/ST (Prevention of Atrocities) Act, 1989 and immediate action.

Enclosures: Caste certificate, evidence, medical report, identity proof

[Date]

problem_description: A citizen suspects or is aware that a person, especially a woman or child, is being trafficked for forced labour, sexual exploitation, or domestic servitude.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Call Police Emergency (112) or Childline (1098) immediately.
Step 2 – Report to the Anti-Human Trafficking Unit (AHTU) of the nearest police station.
Step 3 – File a complaint under the IPC anti-trafficking provisions.
Step 4 – Contact the National Human Rights Commission.
Step 5 – Reach out to NGOs like Rescue Foundation for immediate rescue assistance.
Step 6 – File a complaint with the National Commission for Women for women victims.
Step 7 – Follow up on rehabilitation of rescued victims.
escalation_path: AHTU → CBI (if inter-state/national) → NIA → NHRC

I request immediate rescue operation and rehabilitation.

problem_description: Someone is spreading false and defamatory information about a citizen orally or through social media/print, damaging their reputation.

Step 3 – File a criminal complaint under Section 499/500 IPC for defamation.
Step 4 – For social media defamation, file a complaint on the Cyber Crime Portal.
Step 5 – File a civil suit for damages for defamation.

problem_description: A registered MGNREGA worker demanded employment but was not provided work within 15 days, entitling them to unemployment allowance which is also being denied.

Step 6 – File a complaint on the MGNREGA Ombudsman portal.
Step 7 – File on CPGRAMS if no action is taken.

problem_description: A citizen's passport application has been pending for an abnormally long time, or their passport has been denied without valid reason.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Track application status on the Passport Seva Portal.
Step 2 – Check if police verification is pending and follow up with local police.
Step 3 – File a grievance on the Passport Seva Portal.
Step 4 – Contact the Regional Passport Office.
Step 5 – File a complaint on CPGRAMS.
Step 6 – Contact the MEA Passport Division for escalation.
Step 7 – Approach the High Court for a writ if delay is unreasonable.

Step 3 – File a complaint with the gas company's customer service.
Step 4 – File a complaint with the Petroleum and Natural Gas Regulatory Board (PNGRB).
Step 5 – File a consumer complaint in the District Consumer Commission.
Step 6 – File on CPGRAMS.

subcategory: मकान मालिक द्वारा मूल संपत्ति दस्तावेज वापस करने से इनकार
problem_description: A tenant gave original property documents to a landlord as security at the time of renting, and the landlord is now refusing to return these documents after the tenancy ended.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Send a formal legal notice to the landlord demanding return of documents within 15 days.
Step 2 – File a police complaint for criminal breach of trust (Section 406 IPC).
Step 3 – File a civil suit for recovery of documents.
Step 4 – Apply to the court for an injunction preventing the landlord from misusing the documents.
Step 5 – File a complaint with DLSA for free legal aid.
Step 6 – If the landlord has used the documents fraudulently, add charges of forgery.
required_documents: Proof that documents were given to landlord (rent agreement, witness)
escalation_path: Legal Notice → Police FIR → Civil Court → High Court

I request FIR registration under Section 406 IPC for criminal breach of trust.

Enclosures: Rent agreement, demand notice, identity proof

[Date]

problem_description: A PG resident or flatmate is being asked to leave without notice or is being denied services promised (meals, Wi-Fi, housekeeping) by the PG owner.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Review your PG agreement or rental agreement for notice period and promised services.
Step 2 – Send a written complaint to the PG owner for deficiency of services.
Step 3 – File a consumer complaint for denial of promised PG services.
Step 4 – File a complaint with the local police if forcible eviction is threatened.
Step 5 – Approach the Rent Controller if you have a formal rental agreement.
Step 6 – File a complaint with the Municipal Corporation if the PG is unregistered.
escalation_path: PG Owner → Police → Consumer Commission

subcategory: मकान मालिक द्वारा कानूनी सीमा से अधिक किराया वृद्धि की मांग
problem_description: A landlord is demanding a rent increase that is excessive and beyond the legally permissible limits under the applicable Rent Control Act.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Check the applicable Rent Control Act and permissible rent hike in your state.
Step 2 – Send a written response to the landlord stating the legal rent increase limit.
Step 3 – Continue paying the current legal rent and maintain payment receipts.
Step 4 – File a petition with the Rent Controller for fixation of standard rent.
Step 5 – The Rent Controller will determine the fair/standard rent for the premises.
Step 6 – Seek legal aid from DLSA.
escalation_path: Rent Controller → Civil Court → High Court

I request the Rent Controller to fix the standard rent for my premises.

subcategory: मकान मालिक के गुंडों द्वारा किरायेदार को परेशान करना
problem_description: A landlord has hired goons or antisocial elements to harass, threaten, or intimidate a tenant to force them to vacate outside of the legal eviction process.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Document all harassment incidents with dates and descriptions.
Step 2 – Call Police Emergency (112) immediately when harassment occurs.
Step 3 – File an FIR against the landlord and goons for criminal intimidation.
Step 4 – Apply for a restraining/protection order from the Magistrate Court.
Step 5 – File a complaint with the Rent Controller citing illegal eviction attempts.
Step 6 – File a complaint with the SP if local police are non-responsive.
Step 7 – Approach the High Court for protection order if situation escalates.
escalation_path: Police FIR → Magistrate Court → SP → High Court

I request FIR registration and immediate police protection.

Enclosures: Incident diary, rent agreement, identity proof

[Name]
[Date]

subcategory: मकान मालिक द्वारा संपत्ति कर न भरने से किरायेदार को परेशानी
problem_description: A landlord is not paying property tax, and the municipal authority is pressuring or threatening the tenant to vacate or pay dues despite the tenant not being responsible.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Verify that property tax is the landlord's responsibility (check your rent agreement).
Step 2 – Send a written notice to the landlord to pay the outstanding property tax.
Step 3 – Inform the Municipal Corporation in writing that you are only a tenant and not responsible for property tax.
Step 4 – File a complaint against the landlord with the Rent Controller for breach of landlord obligations.
Step 5 – Approach the Civil Court if municipal actions are affecting your tenancy.
Step 6 – Seek legal aid from DLSA.
escalation_path: Landlord Notice → Rent Controller → Civil Court

The Municipal Commissioner,

Enclosures: Rent agreement, identity proof

[Date]

subcategory: कीट संक्रमण शिकायत (मच्छर/चूहे) – नगर निगम विफलता
problem_description: Residents are suffering from severe mosquito breeding, rat infestation, or other pest problems due to a municipal body's failure to conduct regular pest control and sanitation drives.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Document the problem with photographs.
Step 2 – File a written complaint with the Municipal Health Officer.
Step 3 – File on the city's civic complaint app or portal.
Step 4 – File on CPGRAMS.
Step 5 – Report to the District Collector if a disease outbreak is occurring.
Step 6 – File a PIL in the High Court if municipal failure is systematic and causing health hazard.
Step 7 – Report to the District Medical Officer if malaria/dengue cases are rising.
required_documents: Medical records if disease is spread
escalation_path: Municipal Corporation → District Collector → State Health Department → High Court PIL

I request immediate pest control and sanitation drives in our area.

[Date]

problem_description: Electronic waste (old computers, phones, batteries) is being illegally dumped in open areas or being burned by informal recyclers, causing toxic pollution.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Photograph the dumping site with location and e-waste details.
Step 2 – Report to the State Pollution Control Board.
Step 3 – File a complaint with the Municipal Corporation.
Step 4 – Report to CPCB under the E-Waste Management Rules, 2016.
Step 5 – File a complaint with the National Green Tribunal.
Step 6 – Report to the District Collector.

Step 2 – File a complaint with the Municipal Corporation managing the cremation ground.
Step 3 – File a complaint with the State Pollution Control Board.
Step 4 – Request installation of smokeless electric or gas crematoriums.
Step 5 – Approach the District Collector for mediation.
Step 6 – File with the National Green Tribunal.

workflow_steps: Step 1 – Document the nuisance with photographs and resident complaints.
Step 2 – File a complaint with the Gram Panchayat / Municipal Corporation.
Step 3 – File a complaint with the local police under Section 268 IPC (public nuisance).
Step 4 – Report to the State Pollution Control Board for sanitation violations.
Step 5 – File a complaint with the Municipal Health Officer.
Step 6 – Approach the Sub-Divisional Magistrate for a nuisance order.

problem_description: A wetland, lake, or water body is being filled, encroached upon, or destroyed by real estate developers or government projects, causing ecological damage.

Step 3 – File a complaint with the District Collector.
Step 4 – Report to the State Wetland Authority.
Step 5 – File a complaint with the National Green Tribunal.
Step 6 – File a PIL in the High Court for protection of the water body.
Step 7 – Report to the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.

subcategory: रिश्तेदारों द्वारा पारिवारिक संपत्ति से वरिष्ठ नागरिक को बाहर करना
problem_description: Elderly parents or senior citizens are being driven out of their own home or excluded from family property by their own children or relatives.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – File a complaint under Section 23 of the Senior Citizens Act (revocation of property transfer).
Step 2 – File a complaint with the Sub-Divisional Magistrate under the Senior Citizens Act for eviction order.
Step 3 – The SDM will conduct a hearing and can order the children to restore the senior citizen's possession.
Step 4 – File for maintenance under Section 4 of the Senior Citizens Act.
Step 5 – Call the Senior Citizen Helpline (14567) for immediate support.
Step 6 – Approach DLSA for free legal representation.
required_documents: Property ownership documents or gift deed showing transfer to children
escalation_path: SDM (Senior Citizens Act) → Appellate Tribunal → High Court
complaint_template:

The Sub-Divisional Magistrate,
[Sub-Division]

I, [Your Name], aged [Age], am being driven out of my own home at [Address] by my son/daughter [Name] to whom I had transferred the property. Under Section 23 of the Maintenance and Welfare of Parents and Senior Citizens Act, I request revocation of the property transfer and restoration of my residence.

Enclosures: Property documents, identity proof, witness statements

[Name]
[Date]

problem_description: A senior citizen is being denied entitled concessions in railway tickets, bus fares, electricity bills, or other utilities by service providers.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Verify your entitlement to the specific concession.
Step 2 – Submit a written request with age proof (Aadhaar, Senior Citizen ID card) to the service provider.
Step 3 – If denied, file a written complaint with the service provider's grievance cell.
Step 4 – File a consumer complaint in the District Consumer Commission.
Step 5 – File a complaint on CPGRAMS.
Step 6 – Contact the Senior Citizen Helpline (14567) for guidance.
escalation_path: Service Provider → Consumer Commission → CPGRAMS → High Court

I request the commission to direct [Service Provider] to grant me the applicable concession.

Enclosures: Age proof, concession application, denial letter, identity proof

[Date]

subcategory: वरिष्ठ नागरिक के चिकित्सा मुख्तारनामे का दुरुपयोग
problem_description: A family member or caretaker who was given a medical or general Power of Attorney by a senior citizen is misusing it to make financial transactions or decisions against the senior's interests.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – The senior citizen should revoke the Power of Attorney immediately if capable.
Step 2 – Execute a formal revocation deed at the Sub-Registrar office.
Step 3 – Publish a notice of revocation in a local newspaper.
Step 4 – Inform all banks and institutions about the revocation.
Step 5 – File a police FIR for fraud and breach of trust.
Step 6 – File a civil suit for reversal of unauthorized transactions.
Step 7 – Approach DLSA for free legal aid.
escalation_path: POA Revocation → Police FIR → Civil Court → High Court

I have revoked the POA on [Date]. I request FIR registration and recovery of misused assets.

Enclosures: POA copy, revocation deed, evidence of misuse, bank statements

[Name]
[Date]

subcategory: बुजुर्ग व्यक्ति को आपातकालीन चिकित्सा उपचार से इनकार
problem_description: An elderly person was denied emergency medical treatment by a hospital due to inability to pay advance fees, insufficient insurance documents, or age-based discrimination.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – All hospitals are legally required to provide emergency treatment first without demanding advance payment.
Step 2 – If denied, call the health helpline (104) or ambulance (108) immediately.
Step 3 – File a complaint with the District Medical Officer.
Step 4 – File a complaint with the State Medical Council against the hospital.
Step 5 – File a consumer complaint for deficiency of service.
Step 6 – File a complaint with the National Human Rights Commission.
Step 7 – File an FIR for culpable homicide if death occurs due to denial of treatment.
required_documents: Evidence of denial (written if obtained, witness accounts)
escalation_path: DMO → State Medical Council → Consumer Commission → NHRC → High Court
complaint_template:

The District Medical Officer,
[District]

Subject: Complaint regarding denial of emergency medical treatment to senior citizen

I, [Your Name], report that [Hospital Name] denied emergency treatment to [Senior Citizen Name], aged [Age], on [Date] on grounds of [advance payment / insurance / age]. This is a serious violation of the right to emergency medical care.

I request immediate action against the hospital under the Clinical Establishments Act.

Enclosures: Medical records, evidence of denial, identity proof

[Name]
[Date]

subcategory: द्विविवाह / तलाक के बिना दूसरी शादी
problem_description: A husband or wife has remarried without legally divorcing their first spouse, committing bigamy under Section 494 IPC.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Collect evidence of the second marriage: photographs, invitations, witnesses, registration.
Step 2 – File an FIR under Section 494/495 IPC (bigamy) at the local police station.
Step 3 – File a petition for maintenance in the Family Court.
Step 4 – File for divorce in the Family Court if desired.
Step 5 – Apply for continuation of maintenance and property rights.
Step 6 – Seek free legal aid from DLSA.
Step 7 – Contact the State Women's Commission for additional support.
escalation_path: Police FIR → Family Court → High Court
complaint_template:

The Station House Officer,
[Police Station]

I, [Your Name], am the lawfully wedded spouse of [Accused Name] married on [Date]. My spouse has remarried [Second Spouse Name] on [Date] without obtaining a legal divorce from me. This is bigamy under Section 494 IPC.

I request FIR registration and prosecution of my spouse.

Enclosures: Marriage certificate, evidence of second marriage, identity proof

[Name]
[Date]

subcategory: नाबालिग बच्चे के संरक्षण विवाद (अविवाहित माता-पिता)
problem_description: Unmarried parents or couples in live-in relationships are disputing custody and guardianship of their minor child after separation.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Apply to the Family Court for guardianship under the Guardian and Wards Act, 1890.
Step 2 – The court will consider the best interests of the child as the primary factor.
Step 3 – File for interim custody if the child is in danger.
Step 4 – Request the court to appoint a child welfare officer to assess the situation.
Step 5 – Both parents can be granted visiting rights even if one has primary custody.
Step 6 – Seek free legal aid from DLSA.
Step 7 – Contact Childline (1098) if the child's welfare is at immediate risk.
required_documents: Evidence of relationship and care for the child
Any existing documents about child's custody
escalation_path: Family Court → High Court
complaint_template:

The Family Court,
[District]

I request the court to grant me custody/guardianship with defined visiting rights to the other parent.

Enclosures: Birth certificate, identity proof, relationship evidence

[Name]
[Date]

subcategory: पति/पत्नी / परिवार द्वारा जबरन धर्म परिवर्तन
problem_description: A spouse or family members are forcing a person to convert to another religion against their will as a condition of marriage or continuation of the relationship.

Step 4 – File a petition for protection in the Family Court.
Step 5 – Apply for a protection order under the DV Act if accompanied by domestic violence.
Step 6 – Contact DLSA for free legal aid.
Step 7 – Approach the High Court for a writ for protection of religious freedom (Article 25).

Enclosures: Evidence of coercion, identity proof, marriage certificate

[Name]
[Date]

problem_description: After divorce, spouses are disputing division of jointly owned or matrimonial property including the house, investments, and assets.

problem_description: An employee is being systematically denied promotion due to their religion, caste, or community, while less qualified colleagues of other groups are promoted.

Step 3 – File a complaint with the Labour Commissioner for unfair labour practice.
Step 4 – For public sector employees, file with the Administrative Tribunal.

category: सरकारी योजना और सार्वजनिक सेवा समस्याएं
problem_description: A citizen is being denied Aadhaar enrolment or biometric update at an enrolment centre without valid reason, preventing access to government benefits.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Visit an authorized Aadhaar enrolment centre with valid documents.
Step 2 – If denied, ask for the reason in writing.
Step 3 – File a complaint on the UIDAI portal.
Step 4 – Call the UIDAI helpline (1947).
Step 5 – File a complaint on CPGRAMS.
Step 6 – Approach the District Collector who is the grievance authority for Aadhaar.
required_documents: Written denial from enrolment centre (if obtained)
escalation_path: UIDAI Helpline → UIDAI Regional Office → CPGRAMS → District Collector
complaint_template:

workflow_steps: Step 1 – Stop all further payments immediately.

Step 3 – File a complaint at the local police station for fraud.
Step 4 – File a complaint on the National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal.
Step 5 – File a complaint with the Senior Citizen Helpline (14567).
Step 6 – Alert family members to prevent further loss.
Step 7 – File a complaint with the consumer forum if a registered company is involved.

problem_description: A person is aware that a child (girl below 18 or boy below 21) is about to be married or has been married against their will, violating the Prohibition of Child Marriage Act.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Call Childline (1098) immediately to report the child marriage.
Step 2 – File a complaint with the Child Marriage Prohibition Officer (typically the District Magistrate or SDM).
Step 3 – Police can obtain a court order to stop the marriage before it happens.
Step 4 – File an FIR for violation of the Prohibition of Child Marriage Act.
Step 5 – Contact NCPCR for intervention.
Step 6 – If the marriage has already taken place, the child can apply to the court to have it declared void.
required_documents: Child's age proof (birth certificate, school records)
escalation_path: Childline → Child Marriage Prohibition Officer → Police → NCPCR

Step 6 – Approach the High Court with a writ petition for compensation and action.
Step 7 – Seek free legal aid from DLSA.

problem_description: A family member or caretaker is operating an elderly person's bank account without their proper consent, withdrawing funds for personal use.
workflow_steps: Step 1 – Call the Senior Citizen Helpline (14567) for guidance.
Step 2 – Visit the bank and request account freeze or additional security verification.
Step 3 – File a complaint with the Bank Manager.
Step 4 – File a police complaint for criminal breach of trust.
Step 5 – File a complaint with the Banking Ombudsman.
Step 6 – Revoke any standing instructions or power of attorney given to the family member.
Step 7 – Approach DLSA for free legal assistance.
escalation_path: Bank Manager → Banking Ombudsman → Police → Civil Court
